chapter_ar: كتاب عيادة المريض وتشييع الميت والصلاة عليه وحضور دفنه
chapter_en: The Book of Visiting the Sick
ar: عن البراء بن عازب رضي الله عنهما قال‏:‏ أمرنا رسول الله صلى  الله عليه وسلم  بعيادة المريض، واتباع الجنازة، وتشميت العاطس، وإبرار المقسم، ونصر المظلوم، وإجابة الداعى، وإفشاء السلام” ‏(‏‏(‏متفق عليه‏)‏‏)‏
ar_clean: عن البراء بن عازب رضي الله عنهما قال: أمرنا رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم بعيادة المريض، واتباع الجنازة، وتشميت العاطس، وإبرار المقسم، ونصر المظلوم، وإجابة الداعى، وإفشاء السلام” ((متفق عليه))
matn_ar: عن البراء بن عازب رضي الله عنهما قال‏:‏ أمرنا رسول الله صلى  الله عليه وسلم  بعيادة المريض، واتباع الجنازة، وتشميت العاطس، وإبرار المقسم، ونصر المظلوم، وإجابة الداعى، وإفشاء السلام” ‏(‏‏(‏متفق عليه‏)‏‏)‏
matn_ar_clean: عن البراء بن عازب رضي الله عنهما قال‏:‏ أمرنا رسول الله صلى  الله عليه وسلم  بعيادة المريض، واتباع الجنازة، وتشميت العاطس، وإبرار المقسم، ونصر المظلوم، وإجابة الداعى، وإفشاء السلام” ‏(‏‏(‏متفق عليه‏)‏‏)‏
sunnah_ref_en:
Al-Bara' bin `Azib (May Allah be pleased with them) reported: Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) has ordered us to visit the sick, to follow the funeral (of a dead believer), respond to the sneezer (i.e., by saying to him: Yarhamuk-Allah after he says: Al-hamdu lillah), to help those who vow to fulfill it, to help the oppressed, to accept the invitation extended by the inviter; and to promote greetings (i.e., saying As-Salamu `Alaikum).

.
iwh_en:
Al-Bara' bin `Azib (may Allah be pleased with them) reported: Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) has ordered us to visit the sick, to follow the funeral (of a dead believer), respond to the sneezer (i.e., by saying to him: Yarhamuk-Allah after he says: Al-hamdu lillah), to help those who vow to fulfill it, to help the oppressed, to accept the invitation extended by the inviter; and to promote greetings (i.e., saying As-Salamu `Alaikum).

.
iwh_id:
Al-Bara' bin `Azib (radhiyallahu 'anhum) meriwayatkan: Rasulullah ﷺ has ordered us to visit the sick, to follow the funeral (of a dead believer), respond to the sneezer (i.e., by saying to him: Yarhamuk-Allah setelah he bersabda: Al-hamdu lillah), to help those who vow to fulfill it, to help the oppressed, to accept the invitation extended by the inviter; dan to promote greetings (i.e., saying As-Salamu `Alaikum).

.

chapter_ar: كتاب عيادة المريض وتشييع الميت والصلاة عليه وحضور دفنه
chapter_en: The Book of Visiting the Sick
ar: وعن أبي هريرة رضي الله عنه أن رسول الله صلى  الله عليه وسلم  قال‏:‏ ‏"‏حق المسلم علي المسلم خمس‏:‏ رد السلام، وعيادة المريض، واتباع الجنائز، وإجابة الدعوة، وتشميت العاطس” ‏(‏‏(‏متفق عليه‏)‏‏)‏ ‏.‏
ar_clean: وعن أبي هريرة رضي الله عنه أن رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم قال: "حق المسلم علي المسلم خمس: رد السلام، وعيادة المريض، واتباع الجنائز، وإجابة الدعوة، وتشميت العاطس” ((متفق عليه)) .

isnad_chain.name_en: Abu Hurairah [may Allah be pleased with him]

sunnah_ref_en: Abu Hurairah (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said, "Every Muslim has five rights over another Muslim (i.e., he has to perform five duties for another Muslim): to return the greetings, to visit the sick, to accompany funeral processions, to accept an invitation, to respond to the sneezer .
iwh_en: Abu Hurairah (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "Every Muslim has five rights over another Muslim (i.e., he has to perform five duties for another Muslim): to return the greetings, to visit the sick, to accompany funeral processions, to accept an invitation, to respond to the sneezer .
iwh_id: Abu Hurairah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda, "Every Muslim has five rights over another Muslim (i.e., he has to perform five duties for another Muslim): to return the greetings, to visit the sick, to accompany funeral processions, to accept an invitation, to respond to the sneezer .

chapter_ar: كتاب عيادة المريض وتشييع الميت والصلاة عليه وحضور دفنه
chapter_en: The Book of Visiting the Sick
ar: وعنه قالك قال رسول الله صلى  الله عليه وسلم ‏:‏ ‏"‏عن الله عزو جل يقول يوم القيامة‏:‏ ‏"‏يا ابن آدم مرضت فلم تعدني‏!‏ قال‏:‏ يا رب كيف أعودك وأنت رب العالمين‏؟‏‏!‏ قال‏:‏ أما علمت أن عبدي فلاناً مرض فلم تعده‏؟‏ أما علمت أنك لو عدته لوجدتني عنده‏؟‏ يا ابن آدم استطعمتك فلم تطعمني‏!‏ قال‏:‏ يا رب كيف أطعمك وأنت رب العالمين‏؟‏‏!‏ قال‏:‏ أما علمت أنه استطعمك عبدي فلان فلم تطعمه، أما علمت أنك لو أطعمته لوجدت ذلك عندي‏؟‏ يا ابن آدم استسقيتك فلم تسقني‏!‏ قال‏:‏ يارب كيف أسقيك وأنت رب العالمين‏؟‏‏!‏ قال‏:‏ استسقاك عبدي فلان فلم تسقه‏!‏ أما علمت أنك لو سقيته لوجدت ذلك عندي‏؟‏‏"‏ ‏(‏‏(‏رواه مسلم‏)‏‏)‏‏.‏
ar_clean: وعنه قالك قال رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم : "عن الله عزو جل يقول يوم القيامة: "يا ابن آدم مرضت فلم تعدني! قال: يا رب كيف أعودك وأنت رب العالمين؟! قال: أما علمت أن عبدي فلاناً مرض فلم تعده؟ أما علمت أنك لو عدته لوجدتني عنده؟ يا ابن آدم استطعمتك فلم تطعمني! قال: يا رب كيف أطعمك وأنت رب العالمين؟! قال: أما علمت أنه استطعمك عبدي فلان فلم تطعمه، أما علمت أنك لو أطعمته لوجدت ذلك عندي؟ يا ابن آدم استسقيتك فلم تسقني! قال: يارب كيف أسقيك وأنت رب العالمين؟! قال: استسقاك عبدي فلان فلم تسقه! أما علمت أنك لو سقيته لوجدت ذلك عندي؟" ((رواه مسلم)).
isnad_ar: وعنه قالك
matn_ar: قال رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم : "عن الله عزو جل يقول يوم القيامة: "يا ابن آدم مرضت فلم تعدني! قال: يا رب كيف أعودك وأنت رب العالمين؟! قال: أما علمت أن عبدي فلاناً مرض فلم تعده؟ أما علمت أنك لو عدته لوجدتني عنده؟ يا ابن آدم استطعمتك فلم تطعمني! قال: يا رب كيف أطعمك وأنت رب العالمين؟! قال: أما علمت أنه استطعمك عبدي فلان فلم تطعمه، أما علمت أنك لو أطعمته لوجدت ذلك عندي؟ يا ابن آدم استسقيتك فلم تسقني! قال: يارب كيف أسقيك وأنت رب العالمين؟! قال: استسقاك عبدي فلان فلم تسقه! أما علمت أنك لو سقيته لوجدت ذلك عندي؟" ((رواه مسلم)).
matn_ar_clean: قال رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم : "عن الله عزو جل يقول يوم القيامة: "يا ابن آدم مرضت فلم تعدني! قال: يا رب كيف أعودك وأنت رب العالمين؟! قال: أما علمت أن عبدي فلانا مرض فلم تعده؟ أما علمت أنك لو عدته لوجدتني عنده؟ يا ابن آدم استطعمتك فلم تطعمني! قال: يا رب كيف أطعمك وأنت رب العالمين؟! قال: أما علمت أنه استطعمك عبدي فلان فلم تطعمه، أما علمت أنك لو أطعمته لوجدت ذلك عندي؟ يا ابن آدم استسقيتك فلم تسقني! قال: يارب كيف أسقيك وأنت رب العالمين؟! قال: استسقاك عبدي فلان فلم تسقه! أما علمت أنك لو سقيته لوجدت ذلك عندي؟" ((رواه مسلم)).
sunnah_ref_en: Abu Hurairah (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said, "Verily, Allah, the Exalted, and Glorious will say on the Day of Resurrection: 'O son of Adam, I was ill but you did not visit Me.' He would say: 'O my Rubb, how could I visit you and You are the Rubb of the worlds?' Thereupon He would say: 'Did you not know that such and such a slave of Mine was ill but you did not visit him? Did you not realize that if you had visited him (you would have known that I was aware of your visit to him, for which I would reward you) you would have found Me with him? O son of Adam, I asked food from you but you did not feed Me.' He would submit: 'My Rubb, how could I feed You and You are the Rubb of the worlds?' He would say: 'Did you not know that such and such a slave of Mine asked you for food but you did not feed him? Did you not realize that if you had fed him, you would certainly have found (its reward) with Me? O son of Adam, I asked water from you but you did not give it to Me.' He would say: 'My Rubb, how could I give You (water) and You are the Rubb of the worlds?' Thereupon He would say: 'Such and such a slave of Mine asked you for water to drink but you did not give it to him. Did you not realize that if you had given him to drink you would have found (its reward) with Me?"'.
iwh_en: Abu Hurairah (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "Verily, Allah, the Exalted, and Glorious will say on the Day of Resurrection: 'O son of Adam, I was ill but you did not visit Me.' He would say: 'O my Rubb, how could I visit you and You are the Rubb of the worlds?' Thereupon He would say: 'Did you not know that such and such a slave of Mine was ill but you did not visit him? Did you not realize that if you had visited him (you would have known that I was aware of your visit to him, for which I would reward you) you would have found Me with him? O son of Adam, I asked food from you but you did not feed Me.' He would submit: 'My Rubb, how could I feed You and You are the Rubb of the worlds?' He would say: 'Did you not know that such and such a slave of Mine asked you for food but you did not feed him? Did you not realize that if you had fed him, you would certainly have found (its reward) with Me? O son of Adam, I asked water from you but you did not give it to Me.' He would say: 'My Rubb, how could I give You (water) and You are the Rubb of the worlds?' Thereupon He would say: 'Such and such a slave of Mine asked you for water to drink but you did not give it to him. Did you not realize that if you had given him to drink you would have found (its reward) with Me?"'.
iwh_id: Abu Hurairah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ berkata, "Verily, Allah, the Exalted, and Glorious will say on Hari Kebangkitan: 'O putra Adam, I was ill namun you tidak visit Me.' Beliau say: 'O my Rubb, how could I visit you and You are the Rubb dari worlds?' Karenanya Beliau say: 'Did you not mengetahui that sekian dan sekian seorang budak of Mine was ill namun you tidak visit him? Did you not realize that jika kamu had visited him (you would have known that I was aware of your visit to him, for which I would reward you) you would have menemukan Me with him? O putra Adam, Saya bertanya food from you namun you tidak feed Me.' Beliau submit: 'My Rubb, how could I feed You and You are the Rubb dari worlds?' Beliau say: 'Did you not mengetahui that sekian dan sekian seorang budak of Mine bertanya you for food namun you tidak feed him? Did you not realize that jika kamu had fed him, you would certainly have menemukan (its reward) with Me? O putra Adam, Saya bertanya water from you namun you tidak memberikan it to Me.' Beliau say: 'My Rubb, how could I memberikan You (water) and You are the Rubb dari worlds?' Karenanya Beliau say: 'Such dan such seorang budak of Mine bertanya you for water to drink namun you tidak memberikan it to him. Did you not realize that jika kamu had given him to drink you would have menemukan (its reward) with Me?"'.

chapter_ar: كتاب عيادة المريض وتشييع الميت والصلاة عليه وحضور دفنه
chapter_en: The Book of Visiting the Sick
ar: وعن أبي موسي الأشعري رضي الله عنه قال‏:‏ قال رسول الله صلى  الله عليه وسلم ، “ عودوا المريض، وأطعموا الجائع، وفكوا العاني‏"‏ ‏(‏‏(‏رواه البخاري‏)‏‏)‏‏.‏

sunnah_ref_en:
Abu Musa (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said, "Visit the sick, feed the hungry, and (arrange for the) release of the captive.''

.
iwh_en:
Abu Musa (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "Visit the sick, feed the hungry, and (arrange for the) release of the captive.''

.
iwh_id:
Abu Musa (radhiyallahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda, "Visit the sick, feed the hungry, and (arrange for the) release of the captive.''

chapter_ar: كتاب عيادة المريض وتشييع الميت والصلاة عليه وحضور دفنه
chapter_en: The Book of Visiting the Sick
ar: وعن ثوبان، رضي الله عنه، عن النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم الله عليه وسلم  الله عليه وسلم ، قال‏:‏ ‏"‏إن المسلم إذا عاد أخاه المسلم لم يزل في خرفة الجنة حتي يرجع‏"‏ قيل‏:‏ يا رسول الله وما خرفة الجنة‏؟‏ قال‏:‏ ‏"‏جناها” ‏(‏‏(‏رواه مسلم‏)‏‏)‏‏.‏

sunnah_ref_en: Thauban (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "He who visits his brother in Faith, will remain engaged in picking the fresh fruits from the garden of Jannah till he returns.".
iwh_en: Thauban (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that the Prophet ﷺ said, "He who visits his brother in Faith, will remain engaged in picking the fresh fruits from the garden of Jannah till he returns.".
iwh_id: Thauban (radhiyallahu 'anhu) melaporkan bahwa Nabi ﷺ berkata, "Barangsiapa yang visits saudaranya in iman, will remain engaged in picking the fresh fruits from the garden of Jannah till he returns.".

chapter_ar: كتاب عيادة المريض وتشييع الميت والصلاة عليه وحضور دفنه
chapter_en: The Book of Visiting the Sick
ar: وعن علي رضي الله عنه قال‏:‏ سمعت رسول الله صلى  الله عليه وسلم  يقول‏:‏ ‏"‏ما من مسلم يعود مسلماً غدوة إلا صلى  عليه سبعون ألف ملك حتي يمسي، وإن عاده عشية إلا صلى  عليه سبعون ألف ملك حتي يصبح، وكان له خريف في الجنة” ‏(‏‏(‏رواه الترمذي وقال‏:‏ حديث حسن‏)‏‏)‏‏.‏
ar_clean: وعن علي رضي الله عنه قال: سمعت رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم يقول: "ما من مسلم يعود مسلماً غدوة إلا صلى عليه سبعون ألف ملك حتي يمسي، وإن عاده عشية إلا صلى عليه سبعون ألف ملك حتي يصبح، وكان له خريف في الجنة” ((رواه الترمذي وقال: حديث حسن)).

matn_ar: ي، وإن عاده عشية إلا صلى عليه سبعون ألف ملك حتي يصبح، وكان له خريف في الجنة” ((رواه الترمذي وقال: حديث حسن)).

sunnah_ref_en: 'Ali bin Abu Talib (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: I heard the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) saying, "When a Muslim visits a sick Muslim at dawn, seventy thousand angels keep on praying for him till dusk. If he visits him in the evening, seventy thousand angels keep on praying for him till the morning; and he will have (his share of) reaped fruits in Jannah.".
iwh_en: 'Ali bin Abu Talib (may Allah be pleased with him) reported: I heard the Messenger of Allah ﷺ saying, "When a Muslim visits a sick Muslim at dawn, seventy thousand angels keep on praying for him till dusk. If he visits him in the evening, seventy thousand angels keep on praying for him till the morning; and he will have (his share of) reaped fruits in Jannah.".
iwh_id: 'Ali bin Abu Talib (radhiyallahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan: I heard Rasulullah ﷺ saying, "When a Muslim visits a sick Muslim at dawn, seventy thousand angels menjaga on praying for him till dusk. If he visits him in the evening, seventy thousand angels menjaga on praying for him till the morning; dan ia will have (his share of) reaped fruits in Jannah.".

chapter_ar: كتاب عيادة المريض وتشييع الميت والصلاة عليه وحضور دفنه
chapter_en: The Book of Visiting the Sick
ar:
وعن أنس رضي الله عنه قال‏:‏ كان غلام يهودي يخدم النبي صلى الله عليه وسلمى الله عليه وسلم الله عليه وسلم  فمرض فأتاه النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم الله عليه وسلم  الله عليه وسلم  يعوده، فقعد عند رأسه فقال له‏:‏ ‏
"‏أسلم‏"‏ فنظر إلي أبيه وهو عنده‏؟‏ فقال‏:‏ أطع أبا القاسم، فأسلم، فخرج النبي صلى الله عليه وسلمى الله عليه وسلم الله عليه وسلم  وهو يقول‏:‏ “الحمد لله الذي أنقذه من النار” ‏(‏‏(‏رواه البخاري‏)‏‏)‏‏.‏
ar_clean:
وعن أنس رضي الله عنه قال: كان غلام يهودي يخدم النبي صلى الله عليه وسلمى الله عليه وسلم الله عليه وسلم فمرض فأتاه النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم الله عليه وسلم الله عليه وسلم يعوده، فقعد عند رأسه فقال له: 
"أسلم" فنظر إلي أبيه وهو عنده؟ فقال: أطع أبا القاسم، فأسلم، فخرج النبي صلى الله عليه وسلمى الله عليه وسلم الله عليه وسلم وهو يقول: “الحمد لله الذي أنقذه من النار” ((رواه البخاري)).
isnad_ar: وعن أنس رضي الله عنه قال: كان غلام يهودي يخدم النبي صلى الله عليه وسلمى الله عليه وسلم الله عليه وسلم فمرض فأتاه النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم الله عليه وسلم الله عليه وسلم يعوده

فقعد عند رأسه فقال له:

sunnah_ref_en:
Anas (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: A young Jewish boy who was in the service of the Prophet (ﷺ) fell ill. The Prophet (ﷺ) went to visit him. He sat down by his head and said to him, "Embrace Islam." The little boy looked at his father who was sitting beside him. He said: "Obey Abul-Qasim (i.e., the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ))." So he embraced Islam and the Prophet (ﷺ) stepped out saying, "Praise be to Allah Who has saved
him from Hell-fire."

.

Anas (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that a young Jewish boy who was in the service of the Prophet ﷺ fell ill. the Prophet ﷺ went to visit him. He sat down by his head and said to him, "Embrace Islam." The little boy looked at his father who was sitting beside him. He said: "Obey Abul-Qasim (i.e., the Messenger of Allah ﷺ)." So he embraced Islam and the Prophet ﷺ stepped out saying, "Praise be to Allah Who has saved
him from Hell-fire."

.
iwh_id:
Anas (radhiyallahu 'anhu) melaporkan bahwa a muda Jewish boy who was in the service of Nabi ﷺ fell ill. Nabi ﷺ pergi kepada visit him. He duduk down by his head dan bersabda kepadanya, "Embrace Islam." The little boy looked at ayahnya who was sitting beside him. He berkata: "Obey Abul-Qasim (i.e., Rasulullah ﷺ)." Jadi he embraced Islam and Nabi ﷺ stepped out saying, "Praise be to Allah Who has saved
him from Neraka."

.

ar:
عن عائشة رضي الله عنها، أن النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم الله عليه وسلم  الله عليه وسلم  كان إذا اشتكي الأنسان الشيء منه، أو كانت به قرحة أو جرح، قال النبي صلى الله عليه وسلمى الله عليه وسلم الله عليه وسلم  بأصبعه هكذا، ووضع سفيان بن عيينه الراوي سبابته بالأرض ثم رفعها وقال‏:‏ ‏
"‏بسم الله، تربة أرضنا، بريقة بعضنا، يشفي سقيمنا، بإذن ربنا‏"‏ ‏(‏‏(‏متفق عليه‏)‏‏)‏

sunnah_ref_en:
`Aishah (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: When a person complained to the Prophet (ﷺ) about an ailment or suffered from a sore or a wound, the Prophet (ﷺ) would touch the ground with his forefinger and then raise it (Sufyan bin `Uyainah, the narrator, demonstrated this with his forefinger) and would recite: `Bismil-lahi, turbatu ardina, biriqati ba`dina, yushfa bihi saqimuni, bi 'idhni Rabbina' (With the Name of Allah, the dust of our ground mixed with the saliva of some of us would cure our patient with the permission of our Rubb.''

.

`Aishah (may Allah be pleased with him) reported: When a person complained to the Prophet ﷺ about an ailment or suffered from a sore or a wound, the Prophet ﷺ would touch the ground with his forefinger and then raise it (Sufyan bin `Uyainah, the narrator, demonstrated this with his forefinger) and would recite: `Bismil-lahi, turbatu ardina, biriqati ba`dina, yushfa bihi saqimuni, bi 'idhni Rabbina' (With the Name of Allah, the dust of our ground mixed with the saliva of some of us would cure our patient with the permission of our Rubb.''

.
iwh_id:
`Aishah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan: When seseorang complained to Nabi ﷺ tentang an ailment or suffered from a sore or a wound, Nabi ﷺ would touch the ground with his forefinger dan kemudian raise it (Sufyan bin `Uyainah, the narrator, demonstrated this with his forefinger) and would membaca: `Bismil-lahi, turbatu ardina, biriqati ba`dina, yushfa bihi saqimuni, bi 'idhni Rabbina' (With the Name of Allah, the dust of our ground mixed with the saliva of some of us would cure our patient with the permission of our Rubb.''

.

chapter_ar: كتاب عيادة المريض وتشييع الميت والصلاة عليه وحضور دفنه

ar:

"‏اللهم اشف سعداً، اللهم اشف سعداً، اللهم اشف سعداً‏"‏ ‏(‏‏(‏رواه مسلم‏)‏‏)‏‏.‏

chapter_ar: كتاب عيادة المريض وتشييع الميت والصلاة عليه وحضور دفنه
chapter_en: The Book of Visiting the Sick
ar:
وعن ابن عباس، رضي الله عنهما، عن النبي،صلى الله عليه وسلمى الله عليه وسلم صلى  الله عليه وسلم ، قال‏:‏ ‏
"‏من عاد مريضاً لم يحضره أجله، فقال عنده سبع مرات‏:‏ أسأل الله العظيم رب العرش العظيم أن يشفيك‏:‏ إلا عافاه الله من ذلك المرض‏"‏ ‏(‏‏(‏رواه أبو داود والترمذي وقال‏:‏ حديث حسن، وقال الحاكم‏:‏ حديث صحيح علي شرط البخاري‏)‏‏)‏‏.‏

"من عاد مريضا لم يحضره أجله، فقال عنده سبع مرات: أسأل الله العظيم رب العرش العظيم أن يشفيك: إلا عافاه الله من ذلك المرض" ((رواه أبو داود والترمذي وقال: حديث حسن، وقال الحاكم: حديث صحيح علي شرط البخاري)).
sunnah_ref_en: Ibn 'Abbas (May Allah be pleased with them) reported: The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "He who visits a sick person who is not on the point of death and supplicates seven times: As'alullahal-'Azima Rabbal-'Arshil-'Azimi, an yashfiyaka (I beseech Allah the Great, the Rubb of the Great Throne, to heal you), Allah will certainly heal him from that sickness.".
iwh_en: Ibn 'Abbas (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that the Prophet ﷺ said, "He who visits a sick person who is not on the point of death and supplicates seven times: As'alullahal-'Azima Rabbal-'Arshil-'Azimi, an yashfiyaka (I beseech Allah the Great, the Rubb of the Great Throne, to heal you), Allah will certainly heal him from that sickness.".
iwh_id: Ibn 'Abbas (radhiyallahu 'anhu) melaporkan bahwa Nabi ﷺ berkata, "Barangsiapa yang visits a sick person who bukan on the point of death dan supplicates seven times: As'alullahal-'Azima Rabbal-'Arshil-'Azimi, an yashfiyaka (I beseech Allah the Great, the Rubb of the Great Throne, to heal you), Allah will certainly heal him from that sickness.".

ar:
وعنه أن النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم الله عليه وسلم صلى  الله عليه وسلم ، دخل علي أعرأبي يعوده وكان إذا دخل علي من يعوده قال‏:‏ ‏
"‏لا بأس، طهور إن شاء الله‏"‏ ‏(‏‏(‏رواه البخاري‏)‏‏)‏‏.‏

ar:

"‏نعم‏"‏ قال‏:‏ بسم الله أرقيك ، من كل شيء يؤذيك، ومن شر كل نفس أو عين حاسد، الله يشفيك، بسم الله أرقيك” ‏(‏‏(‏رواه مسلم‏)‏‏)‏‏.‏

Jibril supplicated thus (i.e., he performed Ruqyah): "Bismillahi arqika, min kulli shay'in yu'dhika, min sharri kulli nafsin aw 'ayni hasidi, Allahu yashfika, bismillahi arqika. .
iwh_en: Abu Sa'id Al-Khudri (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that jibril (Gabriel) came to the Prophet ﷺ and said: "O Muhammad (ﷺ)! Do you feel sick?" He (ﷺ) said, "Yes." Jibril supplicated thus (i.e., he performed Ruqyah): "Bismillahi arqika, min kulli shay'in yu'dhika, min sharri kulli nafsin aw 'ayni hasidi, Allahu yashfika, bismillahi arqika. .
iwh_id: Abu Sa'id Al-Khudri (radhiyallahu 'anhu) melaporkan bahwa jibril (Gabriel) datang kepada Nabi ﷺ dan berkata: "O Muhammad (ﷺ)! Do you feel sick?" He (ﷺ) berkata, "Yes." Jibril supplicated dengan demikian (i.e., he performed Ruqyah): "Bismillahi arqika, min kulli shay'in yu'dhika, min sharri kulli nafsin aw 'ayni hasidi, Allahu yashfika, bismillahi arqika. .

chapter_ar: كتاب عيادة المريض وتشييع الميت والصلاة عليه وحضور دفنه
chapter_en: The Book of Visiting the Sick
ar: وعن أبي سعيد الخدري وأبي هريرة، رضي الله عنهما، أنهما شهدا علي رسول الله ، صلى  الله عليه وسلم ، أنه قال‏:‏ ‏"‏من قال‏:‏ لا إله إلا الله والله اكبر، صدقه ربه، فقال‏:‏ لا إله إلا أنا وأنا أكبر‏.‏ وإذا قال‏:‏ لا إله إلا الله وحده لا شريك له، قال‏:‏ يقول‏:‏ لا إله إلا أنا وحدي لا شريك لي‏.‏ وإذا قال‏:‏ لا إله إلا الله له الملك وله الحمد، قال‏:‏ لا إله إلا أنا لي الملك ولي الحمد‏.‏ وإذا قال‏:‏ لا إله إلا الله ولا حول ولا قوة إلا بالله، قال‏:‏ لا إله إلا أنا ولا حول ولا قوة إلا بي‏"‏ وكان يقول‏:‏ ‏"‏من قالها في مرضه ثم مات لم تطعمه النار‏"‏ ‏(‏‏(‏رواه الترمذي وقال‏:‏ حديث حسن‏)‏‏)‏

isnad_ar: وعن أبي سعيد الخدري وأبي هريرة، رضي الله عنه
matn_ar: ما، أنهما شهدا علي رسول الله ، صلى الله عليه وسلم ، أنه قال: "من قال: لا إله إلا الله والله اكبر، صدقه ربه، فقال: لا إله إلا أنا وأنا أكبر. وإذا قال: لا إله إلا الله وحده لا شريك له، قال: يقول: لا إله إلا أنا وحدي لا شريك لي. وإذا قال: لا إله إلا الله له الملك وله الحمد، قال: لا إله إلا أنا لي الملك ولي الحمد. وإذا قال: لا إله إلا الله ولا حول ولا قوة إلا بالله، قال: لا إله إلا أنا ولا حول ولا قوة إلا بي" وكان يقول: "من قالها في مرضه ثم مات لم تطعمه النار" ((رواه الترمذي وقال: حديث حسن))
matn_ar_clean: ما، أنهما شهدا علي رسول الله ، صلى الله عليه وسلم ، أنه قال: "من قال: لا إله إلا الله والله اكبر، صدقه ربه، فقال: لا إله إلا أنا وأنا أكبر. وإذا قال: لا إله إلا الله وحده لا شريك له، قال: يقول: لا إله إلا أنا وحدي لا شريك لي. وإذا قال: لا إله إلا الله له الملك وله الحمد، قال: لا إله إلا أنا لي الملك ولي الحمد. وإذا قال: لا إله إلا الله ولا حول ولا قوة إلا بالله، قال: لا إله إلا أنا ولا حول ولا قوة إلا بي" وكان يقول: "من قالها في مرضه ثم مات لم تطعمه النار" ((رواه الترمذي وقال: حديث حسن))
sunnah_ref_en: Abu Sa'id Al-Khudri and Abu Hurairah (May Allah be pleased with them) reported: The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said, "If a person says: 'La ilaha illallahu wallahu Akbar (There is no true god except Allah and Allah is Greatest)', his Rubb responds to him and affirms: '(Yes!) There is no true god except I, and I am the Greatest.' When he says: 'La ilaha illallahu Wahdahu la sharika Lahu (There is no true god except Allah, the One, He has no partner).' Allah (SWT) affirms: '(Yes!) There is no true god except I. I have no partner.' When he says: 'La ilaha illallahu Lahul mulku wa Lahul-hamdu (the sovereignty belongs to Him and all the praise is due to Him).' He (SWT) affirms: '(Yes!) There is no true god except I, Mine is the praise and to Me belongs the sovereignty.' When he says: 'La ilaha illallahu wa la hawla wa la quwwata illa Billah (There is no true god except Allah, and there is no might and power but with Allah).' He (SWT) affirms: '(Yes!) There is no true god except I, and there is no might and power but with Me."' The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) added, "He who recites this during his illness and dies, will not be touched by the Fire (Hell).".
iwh_en: Abu Sa'id Al-Khudri and Abu Hurairah (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "If a person says: 'La ilaha illallahu wallahu Akbar (There is no true god except Allah and Allah is Greatest)', his Rubb responds to him and affirms: '(Yes!) There is no true god except I, and I am the Greatest.' When he says: 'La ilaha illallahu Wahdahu la sharika Lahu (There is no true god except Allah, the One, He has no partner).' Allah (SWT) affirms: '(Yes!) There is no true god except I. I have no partner.' When he says: 'La ilaha illallahu Lahul mulku wa Lahul-hamdu (the sovereignty belongs to Him and all the praise is due to Him).' He (SWT) affirms: '(Yes!) There is no true god except I, Mine is the praise and to Me belongs the sovereignty.' When he says: 'La ilaha illallahu wa la hawla wa la quwwata illa Billah (There is no true god except Allah, and there is no might and power but with Allah).' He (SWT) affirms: '(Yes!) There is no true god except I, and there is no might and power but with Me."' the Messenger of Allah ﷺ added, "He who recites this during his illness and dies, will not be touched by the Fire (Hell).".
iwh_id: Abu Sa'id Al-Khudri and Abu Hurairah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ berkata, "If seseorang berkata: 'La ilaha illallahu wallahu Akbar (There is no true god except Allah and Allah is Greatest)', his Rubb responds to him dan affirms: '(Yes!) There is no true god except I, dan aku am the Greatest.' Ketika ia berkata: 'La ilaha illallahu Wahdahu la sharika Lahu (There is no true god except Allah, the One, He has no partner).' Allah (SWT) affirms: '(Yes!) There is no true god except I. I have no partner.' Ketika ia berkata: 'La ilaha illallahu Lahul mulku wa Lahul-hamdu (the sovereignty belongs to Him dan all the praise is due to Him).' He (SWT) affirms: '(Yes!) There is no true god except I, Mine is the praise dan to Me belongs the sovereignty.' Ketika ia berkata: 'La ilaha illallahu wa la hawla wa la quwwata illa Billah (There is no true god except Allah, and there is no might dan power namun with Allah).' He (SWT) affirms: '(Yes!) There is no true god except I, and there is no might dan power namun with Me."' Rasulullah ﷺ added, "Barangsiapa yang recites this selama his illness dan dies, tidak akan be touched oleh Fire (Neraka).".

chapter_ar: كتاب عيادة المريض وتشييع الميت والصلاة عليه وحضور دفنه
chapter_en: The Book of Visiting the Sick
ar: عن ابن عباس، رضي الله عنهما، أن علي بن أبي طالب، رضي الله عنه خرج من عند رسول الله صلى  الله عليه وسلم ، في وجعه الذي توفي فيه فقال الناس‏:‏ يا أبا الحسن كيف أصبح رسول الله صلى  الله عليه وسلم ‏؟‏ قال‏:‏ أصبح بحمد الله بارئاً‏"‏ ‏(‏‏(‏رواه البخاري‏)‏‏)‏‏.‏
ar_clean: عن ابن عباس، رضي الله عنهما، أن علي بن أبي طالب، رضي الله عنه خرج من عند رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم ، في وجعه الذي توفي فيه فقال الناس: يا أبا الحسن كيف أصبح رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم ؟ قال: أصبح بحمد الله بارئاً" ((رواه البخاري)).

matn_ar: قال الناس: يا أبا الحسن كيف أصبح رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم ؟ قال: أصبح بحمد الله بارئاً" ((رواه البخاري)).
matn_ar_clean: قال الناس: يا أبا الحسن كيف أصبح رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم ؟ قال: أصبح بحمد الله بارئا" ((رواه البخاري)).
sunnah_ref_en:
Ibn `Abbas (May Allah be pleased with them) reported: When `Ali (May Allah be pleased with him) came out after visiting the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) during his last illness, the people asked: "How is Messenger of Allah (ﷺ), O Abul-Hasan?'' He replied: "Praise be to Allah, he (ﷺ) is feeling better.''

.
iwh_en:
Ibn `Abbas (may Allah be pleased with them) reported: When `Ali (may Allah be pleased with him) came out after visiting the Messenger of Allah ﷺ during his last illness, the people asked: "How is Messenger of Allah (ﷺ), O Abul-Hasan?'' He replied: "Praise be to Allah, he (ﷺ) is feeling better.''

.
iwh_id:
Ibn `Abbas (radhiyallahu 'anhum) meriwayatkan: When `Ali (radhiyallahu 'anhu) came out setelah visiting Rasulullah ﷺ selama his terakhir illness, the orang-orang bertanya: "How is Rasulullah ﷺ, O Abul-Hasan?'' Ia menjawab: "Praise be to Allah, he (ﷺ) is feeling better.''

.

chapter_ar: كتاب عيادة المريض وتشييع الميت والصلاة عليه وحضور دفنه
chapter_en: The Book of Visiting the Sick
ar: عن عائشة رضي الله عنها قالت‏:‏ سمعت النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم الله عليه وسلم  الله عليه وسلم  وهو مستند إلي يقول‏:‏ اللهم اغفر لي وارحمني وألحقني بالرفيق الأعلى‏"‏ ‏(‏‏(‏متفق عليه‏)‏‏)‏‏.‏
ar_clean: عن عائشة رضي الله عنها قالت: سمعت النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم الله عليه وسلم الله عليه وسلم وهو مستند إلي يقول: اللهم اغفر لي وارحمني وألحقني بالرفيق الأعلى" ((متفق عليه)).
matn_ar: عن عائشة رضي الله عنها قالت‏:‏ سمعت النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم الله عليه وسلم  الله عليه وسلم  وهو مستند إلي يقول‏:‏ اللهم اغفر لي وارحمني وألحقني بالرفيق الأعلى‏"‏ ‏(‏‏(‏متفق عليه‏)‏‏)‏‏.‏
matn_ar_clean: عن عائشة رضي الله عنها قالت‏:‏ سمعت النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم الله عليه وسلم  الله عليه وسلم  وهو مستند إلي يقول‏:‏ اللهم اغفر لي وارحمني وألحقني بالرفيق الأعلى‏"‏ ‏(‏‏(‏متفق عليه‏)‏‏)‏‏.‏
sunnah_ref_en:
`Aishah (May Allah be pleased with her) reported: While the Prophet (ﷺ) was reclining against me (during his last illness) I heard him saying: "Allahumma-ghfir li, warhamni, wa alhiqni bir Rafiqil-A`la (O Allah, forgive me, bestow Your Mercy on me and let me join with the exalted companions.''

.
iwh_en:
`Aishah (may Allah be pleased with her) reported: While the Prophet ﷺ was reclining against me (during his last illness) I heard him saying: "Allahumma-ghfir li, warhamni, wa alhiqni bir Rafiqil-A`la (O Allah, forgive me, bestow Your Mercy on me and let me join with the exalted companions.''

.
iwh_id:
`Aishah (radhiyallahu 'anha) meriwayatkan: While Nabi ﷺ was reclining terhadap me (selama his terakhir illness) Saya mendengar him saying: "Allahumma-ghfir li, warhamni, wa alhiqni bir Rafiqil-A`la (Ya Allah, forgive me, bestow Your Mercy on me dan let me join with the exalted companions.''

.

chapter_ar: كتاب عيادة المريض وتشييع الميت والصلاة عليه وحضور دفنه

"‏اللهم أعني علي غمرات الموت وسكرات الموت‏"‏ ‏(‏‏(‏رواه الترمذي‏)‏‏)‏‏.‏
sunnah_ref_en: 'Aishah (May Allah be pleased with her) reported: I saw the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) when he was at the point of death. He had a drinking bowl with water in it. He would dip his hand into the bowl, and wipe his face. Then he would supplicate, "O Allah! Help me over pangs and agony of death.".
iwh_en: 'Aishah (may Allah be pleased with her) reported: I saw the Messenger of Allah ﷺ when he was at the point of death. He had a drinking bowl with water in it. He would dip his hand into the bowl, and wipe his face. Then he would supplicate, "O Allah! Help me over pangs and agony of death.".
iwh_id:
'Aishah (radhiyallahu 'anha) meriwayatkan:
Saya melihat Rasulullah ﷺ ketika ia sedang at the point of death. He had a drinking bowl with water in it. Beliau dip his hand into the bowl, and wipe his face. Kemudian ia supplicate, "Ya Allah! Help me over pangs dan agony of death.".

chapter_ar: كتاب عيادة المريض وتشييع الميت والصلاة عليه وحضور دفنه
chapter_en: The Book of Visiting the Sick
ar:
عن عمران بن الحصين رضي الله عنهما أن امراة من جهينة أتت النبي صلى الله عليه وسلمى الله عليه وسلم الله عليه وسلم الله عليه وسلم  وهي حبلي من الزنا، فقالت‏:‏ يا رسول الله، أصبت حداً فأقمه علي ، فدعا رسول الله صلى  الله عليه وسلم  وليها، فقال‏:‏ ‏
"‏أحسن إليها فإذا وضعت فأتني بها” ففعل، فأمر بها النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم الله عليه وسلمى الله عليه وسلم  الله عليه وسلم ، فشدت عليها ثيابها، ثم أمر بها فرجمت، ثم صلى  عليها‏"‏ ‏(‏‏(‏رواه مسلم‏)‏‏)‏‏.‏
ar_clean:

"أحسن إليها فإذا وضعت فأتني بها” ففعل، فأمر بها النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم الله عليه وسلمى الله عليه وسلم الله عليه وسلم ، فشدت عليها ثيابها، ثم أمر بها فرجمت، ثم صلى عليها" ((رواه مسلم)).

"‏أحسن إليها فإذا وضعت فأتني بها” ففعل، فأمر بها النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم الله عليه وسلمى الله عليه وسلم  الله عليه وسلم ، فشدت عليها ثيابها، ثم أمر بها فرجمت، ثم صلى  عليها‏"‏ ‏(‏‏(‏رواه مسلم‏)‏‏)‏‏.‏

"‏أحسن إليها فإذا وضعت فأتني بها” ففعل، فأمر بها النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم الله عليه وسلمى الله عليه وسلم  الله عليه وسلم ، فشدت عليها ثيابها، ثم أمر بها فرجمت، ثم صلى  عليها‏"‏ ‏(‏‏(‏رواه مسلم‏)‏‏)‏‏.‏
sunnah_ref_en:
`Imran bin Husain (May Allah be pleased with them) reported: A woman belonging to the Juhainah tribe came to the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) after having conceived from Zina. She submitted: "O Messenger of Allah! I am liable to Hadd (punishment ordained by Allah), so execute it.'' The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) called her guardian and said, "Treat her well and bring her to me after delivery.'' He acted accordingly. Then the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) commanded to tie her clothes firmly around her and then stoned her to death. He (ﷺ) then offered funeral prayer for her.

.
iwh_en:
`Imran bin Husain (may Allah be pleased with them) reported: A woman belonging to the Juhainah tribe came to the Messenger of Allah ﷺ after having conceived from Zina. She submitted: "O Messenger of Allah! I am liable to Hadd (punishment ordained by Allah), so execute it.'' the Messenger of Allah ﷺ called her guardian and said, "Treat her well and bring her to me after delivery.'' He acted accordingly. Then the Messenger of Allah ﷺ commanded to tie her clothes firmly around her and then stoned her to death. He (ﷺ) then offered funeral prayer for her.

.
iwh_id:
`Imran bin Husain (radhiyallahu 'anhum) meriwayatkan: A woman belonging to the Juhainah tribe came to Rasulullah ﷺ setelah having conceived from Zina. She submitted: "O Rasulullah! I am liable to Hadd (punishment ordained by Allah), jadi execute it.'' Rasulullah ﷺ called her guardian dan bersabda, "Treat her well dan bring her to me setelah delivery.'' He acted accordingly. Kemudian Rasulullah ﷺ memerintahkan to tie her clothes firmly sekitar her dan kemudian stoned her to death. He (ﷺ) kemudian offered funeral prayer for her.

.

chapter_ar: كتاب عيادة المريض وتشييع الميت والصلاة عليه وحضور دفنه
chapter_en: The Book of Visiting the Sick
ar:
عن بن مسعود رضي الله عنه قال‏:‏ دخلت علي النبي صلى الله عليه وسلمى الله عليه وسلم الله عليه وسلم الله عليه وسلم  وهو يوعك، فمسسته، فقلت‏:‏ إنك لتوعك وعكاً شديداً فقال‏:‏ ‏
"‏إني أوعك كما يوعك رجلان منكم‏"‏ ‏(‏‏(‏متفق عليه‏)‏‏)‏

Ibn Mas`ud (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: I visited the Prophet ({ﷺ) while he was suffering. I said: "(O Messenger of Allah!) You suffer too much.'' He said, "Yes, I suffer as much as two men of you.''

.
iwh_en:
Ibn Mas`ud (may Allah be pleased with him) reported: I visited the Prophet ﷺ ({ﷺ) while he was suffering. I said: "(O Messenger of Allah!) You suffer too much.'' He said, "Yes, I suffer as much as two men of you.''

.
iwh_id:
Ibn Mas`ud (radhiyallahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan:
I visited Nabi ﷺ ({ﷺ) while he was suffering. Aku berkata: "(O Rasulullah!) You suffer too much.'' He berkata, "Yes, I suffer as much as two men of you.''

.

chapter_ar: كتاب عيادة المريض وتشييع الميت والصلاة عليه وحضور دفنه
chapter_en: The Book of Visiting the Sick
ar: وعن سعد بن أبي وقاص رضي الله عنه قال‏:‏ جاءني رسول الله صلى  الله عليه وسلم  يعودني من وجع اشتد بي، فقلت‏:‏ بلغ بي ما ترى، وأنا ذو مال، ولايرثني إلا ابنتي، وذكر الحديث‏"‏ ‏(‏‏(‏متفق عليه‏)‏‏)‏‏.‏
ar_clean: وعن سعد بن أبي وقاص رضي الله عنه قال: جاءني رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم يعودني من وجع اشتد بي، فقلت: بلغ بي ما ترى، وأنا ذو مال، ولايرثني إلا ابنتي، وذكر الحديث" ((متفق عليه)).

sunnah_ref_en: Sa'd bin Abu Waqqas (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) came to visit me when I had a severe pain. I said: "I am suffering from such trouble as you are observing. I am a wealthy man and the only heir of mine is my daughter." (Then Sa'd narrated the whole incident)..
iwh_en: Sa'd bin Abu Waqqas (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ came to visit me when I had a severe pain. I said: "I am suffering from such trouble as you are observing. I am a wealthy man and the only heir of mine is my daughter." (Then Sa'd narrated the whole incident)..
iwh_id: Sa'd bin Abu Waqqas (radhiyallahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ datang kepada visit me when I had a severe pain. Aku berkata: "I am suffering from such trouble as you are observing. I am a wealthy man dan the only heir of mine is my putri." (Kemudian Sa'd meriwayatkan the whole incident)..

chapter_ar: كتاب عيادة المريض وتشييع الميت والصلاة عليه وحضور دفنه
chapter_en: The Book of Visiting the Sick
ar: وعن القاسم بن محمد قال‏:‏ قالت عائشة رضي الله عنها‏:‏ وا رأساه فقال النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم الله عليه وسلمى الله عليه وسلم  الله عليه وسلم ‏:‏ ‏"‏بل أنا وا رأساه‏"‏ وذكر الحديث‏"‏ ‏(‏‏(‏رواه البخاري‏)‏‏)‏‏.‏

sunnah_ref_en: Al-Qasim bin Muhammad reported 'Aishah (May Allah be pleased with her) said: "Oh, my head." (It was hurting due to pain.) Thereupon the Prophet (ﷺ) said, "Rather it is I who says: 'Oh, my headache."' (Then Al-Qasim narrated the complete narration)..
iwh_en: Al-Qasim bin Muhammad reported 'Aishah (may Allah be pleased with her) said: "Oh, my head." (It was hurting due to pain.) Thereupon the Prophet ﷺ said, "Rather it is I who says: 'Oh, my headache."' (Then Al-Qasim narrated the complete narration)..
iwh_id: Al-Qasim bin Muhammad meriwayatkan 'Aishah (radhiyallahu 'anha) berkata: "Oh, my head." (It was hurting due to pain.) Thereupon Nabi ﷺ berkata, "Rather it is I who berkata: 'Oh, my headache."' (Kemudian Al-Qasim meriwayatkan the complete narration)..

chapter_ar: كتاب عيادة المريض وتشييع الميت والصلاة عليه وحضور دفنه
chapter_en: The Book of Visiting the Sick

Mu`adh bin Jabal (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said, "He whose last words are: `La ilaha illallah' (There is no true god except Allah) will enter Jannah.''

.
iwh_en:
Mu`adh bin Jabal (may Allah be pleased with him) reported: the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "He whose last words are: `La ilaha illallah' (There is no true god except Allah) will enter Jannah.''

.

Mu`adh bin Jabal (radhiyallahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan: Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda, "He whose terakhir words are: `La ilaha illallah' (There is no true god except Allah) will masuk ke Jannah.''

.

chapter_en: The Book of Visiting the Sick

iwh_en: Abu Sa'id Al-Khudri (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "Exhort your dying men to recite: 'La ilaha illallah' (There is no true god except Allah).".
iwh_id: Abu Sa'id Al-Khudri (radhiyallahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda, "Exhort your dying men to membaca: 'La ilaha illallah' (There is no true god except Allah).".

chapter_ar: كتاب عيادة المريض وتشييع الميت والصلاة عليه وحضور دفنه
chapter_en: The Book of Visiting the Sick
ar: عن أم سلمة رضي الله عنها قالت‏:‏ دخل رسول الله صلى  الله عليه وسلم  علي أبي سلمه وقد شق بصره فأغمضه، ثم قال‏:‏ ‏"‏إن الروح إذا قبض، تبعه البصر‏"‏ فضج ناس من أهله، فقال‏:‏ ‏"‏لا تدعو علي أنفسكم إلا بخير فإن الملائكة يؤمنون علي ما تقولون” ثم قال‏:‏ “اللهم اغفر لأبي سلمه، وارفع درجته في المهديين،واخلفه في عقبه في الغابرين، واغفر لنا وله يا رب العالمين، وافسح له في قبره، ونور له فيه‏"‏ ‏(‏‏(‏رواه مسلم‏)‏‏)‏‏.‏
ar_clean: عن أم سلمة رضي الله عنها قالت: دخل رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم علي أبي سلمه وقد شق بصره فأغمضه، ثم قال: "إن الروح إذا قبض، تبعه البصر" فضج ناس من أهله، فقال: "لا تدعو علي أنفسكم إلا بخير فإن الملائكة يؤمنون علي ما تقولون” ثم قال: “اللهم اغفر لأبي سلمه، وارفع درجته في المهديين،واخلفه في عقبه في الغابرين، واغفر لنا وله يا رب العالمين، وافسح له في قبره، ونور له فيه" ((رواه مسلم)).

sunnah_ref_en:
Umm Salamah (May Allah be pleased with her) reported: The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) visited Abu Salamah (May Allah be pleased with him) when his eyes were open soon after he died. He closed them (the eyes) for him and said, "When the soul is taken away, the sight follows it." Some members of his family began to weep. He (ﷺ) said: "Do not supplicate for yourselves anything but good, for the angels say 'amin' to what you say." Then he said, "O Allah! Forgive Abu Salamah, raise his rank among those who are rightly-guided and grant him a successor from his descendants who remain behind. Grant him pardon and us, too. O Rubb of the worlds. Make his grave spacious for him and give him light in it."

.
iwh_en:
Umm Salamah (may Allah be pleased with her) reported that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ visited Abu Salamah (may Allah be pleased with him) when his eyes were open soon after he died. He closed them (the eyes) for him and said, "When the soul is taken away, the sight follows it." Some members of his family began to weep. He (ﷺ) said: "Do not supplicate for yourselves anything but good, for the angels say 'amin' to what you say." Then he said, "O Allah! Forgive Abu Salamah, raise his rank among those who are rightly-guided and grant him a successor from his descendants who remain behind. Grant him pardon and us, too. O Rubb of the worlds. Make his grave spacious for him and give him light in it."

.
iwh_id:
Umm Salamah (radhiyallahu 'anha) meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ visited Abu Salamah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) when his eyes were open soon setelah he died. He closed them (the eyes) for him dan bersabda, "When the soul is taken away, the sight follows it." Some members of his family began to weep. He (ﷺ) bersabda: "Jangan supplicate for yourselves anything namun baik, for the angels say 'amin' to what you say." Kemudian he bersabda, "Ya Allah! Forgive Abu Salamah, raise his rank di antara those who are rightly-guided dan grant him a successor from his descendants who remain di belakang. Grant him pardon dan us, too. O Rubb of the worlds. Make his grave spacious for him dan memberikan him light in it."

.

chapter_ar: كتاب عيادة المريض وتشييع الميت والصلاة عليه وحضور دفنه
chapter_en: The Book of Visiting the Sick
ar: -عن أم سلمة رضي الله عنها قالت‏:‏ قال رسول الله صلى  الله عليه وسلم ‏:‏ ‏"‏إذا حضرتم المريض، أو الميت، فقولوا خيرا، فإن الملائكة يؤمنون علي ما تقولون، قالت‏:‏ فلما مات أبو سلمة، أتيت النبي صلى الله عليه وسلمى الله عليه وسلم الله عليه وسلم الله عليه وسلم  فقلت‏:‏ يا رسول الله، إن أبا سلمة قد مات، قال‏:‏ “قولي‏:‏ اللهم اغفر لي وله، واعقبني منه عقبة حسنة‏"‏ فقلت‏:‏ فأعقبني الله من هو خير لي منه‏:‏ محمداً صلى  الله عليه وسلم ‏.‏‏"‏ ((رواه مسلم هكَذا:"إِذا حَضَرْتُمُ المَرِيضَ" أَو"الميِّت"عَلَى الشَّكِّ، رواه أبو داود وغيره:"الميِّت"بلا شَكٍّ)).

((رواه مسلم هكَذا:"إِذا حَضَرْتُمُ المَرِيضَ" أَو"الميِّت"عَلَى الشَّكِّ، رواه أبو داود وغيره:"الميِّت"بلا شَكٍّ)).

((رواه مسلم هكَذا:"إِذا حَضَرْتُمُ المَرِيضَ" أَو"الميِّت"عَلَى الشَّكِّ، رواه أبو داود وغيره:"الميِّت"بلا شَكٍّ)).
matn_ar_clean: قال رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم : "إذا حضرتم المريض، أو الميت، فقولوا خيرا، فإن الملائكة يؤمنون علي ما تقولون، قالت: فلما مات أبو سلمة، أتيت النبي صلى الله عليه وسلمى الله عليه وسلم الله عليه وسلم الله عليه وسلم فقلت: يا رسول الله، إن أبا سلمة قد مات، قال: “قولي: اللهم اغفر لي وله، واعقبني منه عقبة حسنة" فقلت: فأعقبني الله من هو خير لي منه: محمدا صلى الله عليه وسلم ." sunnah_ref_en:
Umm Salamah (May Allah be pleased with her): The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said, "When you visit a sick or a dying person, you should utter good words because the angels say `Amin' at what you say.'' She added: When Abu Salamah (May Allah be pleased with him) died, I came to the Prophet (ﷺ) and said: "O Messenger of Allah, Abu Salamah has died." He (ﷺ) directed me to supplicate thus: "Allahummaghfir li wa lahu, wa a`qibni minhu `uqba hasanatan ." So I supplicated as he directed, and Allah gave me a man who was better for me than Abu Salamah (i.e., the Prophet Muhammad (ﷺ)). (The Prophet (ﷺ) married Umm Salamah afterwards.)

.
iwh_en:
Umm Salamah (may Allah be pleased with her): the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "When you visit a sick or a dying person, you should utter good words because the angels say `Amin' at what you say.'' She added: When Abu Salamah (may Allah be pleased with him) died, I came to the Prophet ﷺ and said: "O Messenger of Allah, Abu Salamah has died." He (ﷺ) directed me to supplicate thus: "Allahummaghfir li wa lahu, wa a`qibni minhu `uqba hasanatan ." So I supplicated as he directed, and Allah gave me a man who was better for me than Abu Salamah (i.e., the Prophet ﷺ Muhammad (ﷺ)). (the Prophet ﷺ married Umm Salamah afterwards.)

.
iwh_id:
Umm Salamah (radhiyallahu 'anha): Rasulullah ﷺ berkata, "When you visit a sick or a dying person, you should utter baik words because the angels say `Amin' at what you say.'' She added: When Abu Salamah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) died, I datang kepada Nabi ﷺ dan berkata: "O Rasulullah, Abu Salamah has died." He (ﷺ) directed me to supplicate dengan demikian: "Allahummaghfir li wa lahu, wa a`qibni minhu `uqba hasanatan ." Jadi I supplicated as he directed, and Allah memberikan me seorang laki-laki who was better for me than Abu Salamah (i.e., Nabi ﷺ Muhammad (ﷺ)). (Nabi ﷺ menikah Umm Salamah kemudian.)

.

chapter_ar: كتاب عيادة المريض وتشييع الميت والصلاة عليه وحضور دفنه
chapter_en: The Book of Visiting the Sick
ar: وعنها قالت‏:‏ سمعت رسول الله صلى  الله عليه وسلم  يقول‏:‏‏"‏ما من عبد تصيبه مصيبه، فيقول إن لله وإنا إليه راجعون‏:‏ اللهم آجرني في مصيبتي، واخلف لي خيرا منها، إلا آجره الله تعالي في مصيبته واخلف له خيراً منها‏.‏ قالت‏:‏ فلما توفي أبو سلمة، قلت كما أمرني رسول الله صلى  الله عليه وسلم  ، فاخلف الله خيراص منه رسول الله صلى  الله عليه وسلم “ ‏(‏‏(‏رواه مسلم‏)‏‏)‏‏.‏
ar_clean: وعنها قالت: سمعت رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم يقول:"ما من عبد تصيبه مصيبه، فيقول إن لله وإنا إليه راجعون: اللهم آجرني في مصيبتي، واخلف لي خيرا منها، إلا آجره الله تعالي في مصيبته واخلف له خيراً منها. قالت: فلما توفي أبو سلمة، قلت كما أمرني رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم ، فاخلف الله خيراص منه رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم “ ((رواه مسلم)).
matn_ar: وعنها قالت‏:‏ سمعت رسول الله صلى  الله عليه وسلم  يقول‏:‏‏"‏ما من عبد تصيبه مصيبه، فيقول إن لله وإنا إليه راجعون‏:‏ اللهم آجرني في مصيبتي، واخلف لي خيرا منها، إلا آجره الله تعالي في مصيبته واخلف له خيراً منها‏.‏ قالت‏:‏ فلما توفي أبو سلمة، قلت كما أمرني رسول الله صلى  الله عليه وسلم  ، فاخلف الله خيراص منه رسول الله صلى  الله عليه وسلم “ ‏(‏‏(‏رواه مسلم‏)‏‏)‏‏.‏
matn_ar_clean: وعنها قالت‏:‏ سمعت رسول الله صلى  الله عليه وسلم  يقول‏:‏‏"‏ما من عبد تصيبه مصيبه، فيقول إن لله وإنا إليه راجعون‏:‏ اللهم آجرني في مصيبتي، واخلف لي خيرا منها، إلا آجره الله تعالي في مصيبته واخلف له خيرا منها‏.‏ قالت‏:‏ فلما توفي أبو سلمة، قلت كما أمرني رسول الله صلى  الله عليه وسلم  ، فاخلف الله خيراص منه رسول الله صلى  الله عليه وسلم “ ‏(‏‏(‏رواه مسلم‏)‏‏)‏‏.‏
sunnah_ref_en: Umm Salamah (May Allah be pleased with her) reported: I heard the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) saying, "When a person suffers from a calamity and utters: 'Inna lillahi wa inna ilaihi raji'un. Allahumma ujurni fi musibati, wakhluf li khairan minha (We belong to Allah and to Him we shall return. O Allah! Compensate me in my affliction, recompense my loss and give me something better in exchange for it), then Allah surely compensates him with reward and better substitute." Umm Salamah (May Allah be pleased with her) said: When Abu Salamah (May Allah be pleased with him) died, I repeated the same supplication as the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) had commanded me (to do). So Allah bestowed upon me a better substitute than him (I was married to Muhammad, the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ))..
iwh_en: Umm Salamah (may Allah be pleased with her) reported that i heard the Messenger of Allah ﷺ saying, "When a person suffers from a calamity and utters: 'Inna lillahi wa inna ilaihi raji'un. Allahumma ujurni fi musibati, wakhluf li khairan minha (We belong to Allah and to Him we shall return. O Allah! Compensate me in my affliction, recompense my loss and give me something better in exchange for it), then Allah surely compensates him with reward and better substitute." Umm Salamah (may Allah be pleased with her) said: When Abu Salamah (may Allah be pleased with him) died, I repeated the same supplication as the Messenger of Allah ﷺ had commanded me (to do). So Allah bestowed upon me a better substitute than him (I was married to Muhammad, the Messenger of Allah ﷺ)..
iwh_id: Umm Salamah (radhiyallahu 'anha) meriwayatkan bahwa i heard Rasulullah ﷺ saying, "When seseorang suffers from a calamity dan utters: 'Inna lillahi wa inna ilaihi raji'un. Allahumma ujurni fi musibati, wakhluf li khairan minha (We belong to Allah dan to Him we shall return. Ya Allah! Compensate me in my affliction, recompense my loss dan memberikan me something better in exchange for it), kemudian Allah surely compensates him with reward dan better substitute." Umm Salamah (radhiyallahu 'anha) bersabda: When Abu Salamah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) died, I repeated the same supplication as Rasulullah ﷺ had memerintahkan me (to do). Jadi Allah bestowed upon me a better substitute than him (I was menikah to Muhammad, Rasulullah ﷺ)..

chapter_ar: كتاب عيادة المريض وتشييع الميت والصلاة عليه وحضور دفنه
chapter_en: The Book of Visiting the Sick
ar: وعن أبي موسي رضي الله عنه أن رسول الله صلى  الله عليه وسلم  قال‏:‏ إذا مات ولد العبد، قال الله تعالي لملائكته‏:‏ قبضتم ولد عبدي، فيقولون‏:‏ نعم، فيقول‏:‏ قبضتم ثمرة فؤاده، فيقولون‏:‏ نعم‏.‏ فيقول‏:‏ ماذا قال عبدي، فيقولون‏:‏ حمدك واسترجع، فيقول الله تعالى‏:‏ ابنوا لعبدي بيتاً في الجنة، وسموه بيت الحمد‏"‏ ‏(‏‏(‏رواه الترمذي وقال‏:‏ حديث حسن‏)‏‏)‏‏.‏

matn_ar_clean: قال الله تعالي لملائكته: قبضتم ولد عبدي، فيقولون: نعم، فيقول: قبضتم ثمرة فؤاده، فيقولون: نعم. فيقول: ماذا قال عبدي، فيقولون: حمدك واسترجع، فيقول الله تعالى: ابنوا لعبدي بيتا في الجنة، وسموه بيت الحمد" ((رواه الترمذي وقال: حديث حسن)).
sunnah_ref_en: Abu Musa Al-Ash'ari (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said, "When a man's child dies, Allah, the Exalted, asks His angels, 'Have you taken out the life of the child of My slave?' and they reply in the affirmative. He (SWT) then asks, 'Have you taken the fruit of his heart?' and they reply in the affirmative. Thereupon He asks, 'What did my slave say?' They say: 'He praised You and said: Inna lillahi wa inna ilaihi raji'un (We belong to Allah and to Him we shall return).' Allah says: 'Build a house for my slave in Jannah and name it Baitul-Hamd (the House of Praise)."'.
iwh_en: Abu Musa Al-Ash'ari (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "When a man's child dies, Allah, the Exalted, asks His angels, 'Have you taken out the life of the child of My slave?' and they reply in the affirmative. He (SWT) then asks, 'Have you taken the fruit of his heart?' and they reply in the affirmative. Thereupon He asks, 'What did my slave say?' They say: 'He praised You and said: Inna lillahi wa inna ilaihi raji'un (We belong to Allah and to Him we shall return).' Allah says: 'Build a house for my slave in Jannah and name it Baitul-Hamd (the House of Praise)."'.
iwh_id: Abu Musa Al-Ash'ari (radhiyallahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda, "When seorang laki-laki's child dies, Allah Subhanahu wa Ta'ala, asks His angels, 'Have you taken out the life of the child of My slave?' dan mereka reply in the affirmative. He (SWT) kemudian asks, 'Have you taken the fruit of his heart?' dan mereka reply in the affirmative. Thereupon He asks, 'What did my slave say?' They say: 'He praised You dan bersabda: Inna lillahi wa inna ilaihi raji'un (We belong to Allah dan to Him we shall return).' Allah bersabda: 'Build a house for my slave in Jannah dan name it Baitul-Hamd (the House of Praise)."'.

chapter_ar: كتاب عيادة المريض وتشييع الميت والصلاة عليه وحضور دفنه
chapter_en: The Book of Visiting the Sick
ar: وعن أبي هريرة رضي الله عنه أن رسول الله صلى  الله عليه وسلم  قال‏:‏ يقول الله تعالى‏:‏ ما لعبدي المؤمن عندي جزاء إذا قبضت صفيه من أهل الدنيا، ثم احتسبه إلا الجنه‏"‏ ‏(‏‏(‏رواه البخاري‏)‏‏)‏‏.‏

isnad_chain.name_en: Abu Hurairah [may Allah be pleased with him]

sunnah_ref_en: Abu Hurairah (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said: "Allah, the Exalted, says: 'I have no reward except Jannah for a believing slave of Mine who shows patience and anticipates My reward when I take away his favourite one from the inhabitants of the world."'.
iwh_en: Abu Hurairah (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "Allah, the Exalted, says: 'I have no reward except Jannah for a believing slave of Mine who shows patience and anticipates My reward when I take away his favourite one from the inhabitants of the world."'.
iwh_id: Abu Hurairah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: "Allah Subhanahu wa Ta'ala, bersabda: 'I have no reward except Jannah for a believing slave of Mine who shows patience dan anticipates My reward when I mengambil away his favourite one from the inhabitants of dunia."'.

chapter_ar: كتاب عيادة المريض وتشييع الميت والصلاة عليه وحضور دفنه
chapter_en: The Book of Visiting the Sick
ar: وعن أسامة بن زيد رضي الله عنهما قال‏:‏ أرسلت إحدي بنات النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم الله عليه وسلمى الله عليه وسلم  الله عليه وسلم  إليه تدعوه وتخبره أن صبياً لها -أو ابناً- في الموت فقال للرسول‏:‏ ارجع إليها فأخبرها أن لله تعالي ما أخذ وله ما أعطى، وكل شئ عنده بأجل مسمى، فمرها، فلتصبر ولتحتسب” وذكر تمام الحديث‏"‏ ‏(‏‏(‏متفق عليه‏)‏‏)‏ ‏.‏

sunnah_ref_en: Usamah bin Zaid (May Allah be pleased with them) reported: A daughter of the Prophet (ﷺ) sent a message to him that her son was at his last breath, and requested him to come to her. The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) sent back the informer saying: "To Allah belongs what He takes and what He gives, and everything has a limited period (in this world). So ask her to endure patiently, and expect the reward of Allah.".
iwh_en: Usamah bin Zaid (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that a daughter of the Prophet ﷺ sent a message to him that her son was at his last breath, and requested him to come to her. the Messenger of Allah ﷺ sent back the informer saying: "To Allah belongs what He takes and what He gives, and everything has a limited period (in this world). So ask her to endure patiently, and expect the reward of Allah.".
iwh_id: Usamah bin Zaid (radhiyallahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan bahwa a daughter of Nabi ﷺ sent a message to him that putranya was at his terakhir breath, and requested him to come to her. Rasulullah ﷺ sent back the informer saying: "To Allah belongs what He takes dan what He gives, and everything has a limited period (di dunia ini). Jadi ask her to endure patiently, and expect the reward of Allah.".

chapter_ar: كتاب عيادة المريض وتشييع الميت والصلاة عليه وحضور دفنه
chapter_en: The Book of Visiting the Sick
ar:
عن ابن عمر رضي الله عنهما أن رسول الله صلى  الله عليه وسلم  عاد سعد بن عباده ومعه عبد الرحمن بن عوف، وسعد بن أبي وقاص، وعبد الله بن مسعود رضي الله عنهم، فبكي رسول الله صلى  الله عليه وسلم ، فلما رأي القوم بكاء رسول الله صلى  الله عليه وسلم  ، بكوا ؛ فقال‏:‏ “ألا تسمعون‏؟‏ إن الله لا يعذب بدمع العين، ولا بحزن القلب، ولكن بهذا أو يرحم‏
"‏ وأشار إلا لسانه‏"‏ ‏(‏‏(‏متفق عليه‏)‏‏)‏‏.‏

sunnah_ref_en: Ibn 'Umar (May Allah be pleased with them) reported: The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) visited Sa'd bin 'Ubadah during his illness. He was accompanied by 'Abdur-Rahman bin 'Auf, Sa'd bin Abu Waqqas and 'Abdullah bin Mas'ud (May Allah be pleased with them). The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) began to weep. When his Companions saw this, their tears also started flowing. He (ﷺ) said, "Do you not hear, Allah does not punish for the shedding of tears or the grief of the heart, but punishes or bestows mercy for the utterances of this (and he pointed to his tongue).".

He was accompanied by 'Abdur-Rahman bin 'Auf, Sa'd bin Abu Waqqas and 'Abdullah bin Mas'ud (may Allah be pleased with them). the Messenger of Allah ﷺ began to weep. When his Companions saw this, their tears also started flowing. He (ﷺ) said, "Do you not hear, Allah does not punish for the shedding of tears or the grief of the heart, but punishes or bestows mercy for the utterances of this (and he pointed to his tongue).".
iwh_id: Ibn 'Umar (radhiyallahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ visited Sa'd bin 'Ubadah selama his illness. He was accompanied by 'Abdur-Rahman bin 'Auf, Sa'd bin Abu Waqqas and 'Abdullah bin Mas'ud (radhiyallahu 'anhum). Rasulullah ﷺ began to weep. When his Companions saw this, their tears juga started flowing. He (ﷺ) bersabda, "Do you not hear, Allah does not menghukum for the shedding of tears or the grief of the heart, namun menghukum or bestows mercy for the utterances of this (dan ia pointed to his tongue).".

chapter_ar: كتاب عيادة المريض وتشييع الميت والصلاة عليه وحضور دفنه
chapter_en: The Book of Visiting the Sick
ar:
وعن أسامة بن زيد رضي الله عنهما أن رسول الله صلى  الله عليه وسلم  رفع إليه ابن ابنته وهو في الموت، ففاض عينا رسول الله صلى  الله عليه وسلم  فقال له سعد‏:‏ ما هذا يا رسول الله‏؟‏‏!‏ قال‏:‏ ‏
"‏هذه رحمة جعلها الله تعالي في قلوب عباده الرحماء‏"‏ ‏(‏‏(‏متفق عليه‏)‏‏)‏ ‏.‏

"هذه رحمة جعلها الله تعالي في قلوب عباده الرحماء" ((متفق عليه)) .

sunnah_ref_en: Usamah bin Zaid (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: A grandson (child of the daughter) of the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) was presented to him while the child was at his last breath. Tears began to flow from the eyes of the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ). Sa'd said to him: "What is this, O Messenger of Allah?" He (ﷺ) said, "It is mercy which Allah has placed in the hearts of his salves. Allah bestows His Mercy on the merciful among His slaves.".

Tears began to flow from the eyes of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ. Sa'd said to him: "What is this, O Messenger of Allah?" He (ﷺ) said, "It is mercy which Allah has placed in the hearts of his salves. Allah bestows His Mercy on the merciful among His slaves.".
iwh_id: Usamah bin Zaid (radhiyallahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan bahwa a grandson (child of the daughter) of Rasulullah ﷺ was presented to him while the child was at his terakhir breath. Tears began to flow from the eyes of Rasulullah ﷺ. Sa'd bersabda to him: "What is this, O Rasulullah?" He (ﷺ) bersabda, "It is mercy which Allah has placed in the hearts of his salves. Allah bestows His Mercy on the merciful di antara His slaves.".

chapter_ar: كتاب عيادة المريض وتشييع الميت والصلاة عليه وحضور دفنه
chapter_en: The Book of Visiting the Sick
ar:
وعن أنس رضي الله عنه أن رسول الله صلى  الله عليه وسلم  دخل علي ابنه إبراهيم رضي الله عنه وهو يجود بنفسه فجعلت عينا رسول الله صلى  الله عليه وسلم  تذرفان ‏.‏ فقال له عبد الرحمن بن عوف‏:‏ وأنت يا رسول الله ‏؟‏‏!‏ فقال‏:‏ “يا ابن عوف إنها رحمة” ثم أتبعها بأخرى، فقال‏:‏”إن العين تدمع والقلب يحزن ، ولا نقول إلا ما يرضي ربنا، وإنا بفراقك يا إبراهيم لمحزونون‏"‏‏.‏ ‏(‏‏(‏رواه البخاري، وروي مسلم بعضه‏)‏‏)‏‏.‏ 
والأحاديث في الباب كثيرة في الصحيح مشهورة والله أعلم‏.‏

((رواه البخاري، وروي مسلم بعضه)). 
والأحاديث في الباب كثيرة في الصحيح مشهورة والله أعلم.

sunnah_ref_en: Anas (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) came to his son Ibrahim (May Allah be pleased with him) when he was breathing his last. The eyes of the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) began shedding tears. 'Abdur-Rahman bin 'Auf (May Allah be pleased with him) said, "O Messenger of Allah, you too weep?" He (ﷺ) said, "O Ibn 'Auf! It is mercy." Then he began to weep and said, "The eyes are shedding tears and the heart is grieved, and we will not say except what pleases our Rubb. O Ibrahim! Indeed we are grieved by your departure.".
iwh_en: Anas (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ came to his son Ibrahim (may Allah be pleased with him) when he was breathing his last. The eyes of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ began shedding tears. 'Abdur-Rahman bin 'Auf (may Allah be pleased with him) said, "O Messenger of Allah, you too weep?" He (ﷺ) said, "O Ibn 'Auf! It is mercy." Then he began to weep and said, "The eyes are shedding tears and the heart is grieved, and we will not say except what pleases our Rubb. O Ibrahim! Indeed we are grieved by your departure.".
iwh_id: Anas (radhiyallahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ datang kepada putranya Ibrahim (radhiyallahu 'anhu) ketika ia sedang breathing his terakhir. The eyes of Rasulullah ﷺ began shedding tears. 'Abdur-Rahman bin 'Auf (radhiyallahu 'anhu) berkata, "O Rasulullah, you too weep?" He (ﷺ) berkata, "O Ibn 'Auf! It is mercy." Kemudian he began to weep dan berkata, "The eyes are shedding tears dan the heart is grieved, dan kami tidak akan say except what pleases our Rubb. O Ibrahim! Indeed we are grieved by your departure.".

chapter_ar: كتاب عيادة المريض وتشييع الميت والصلاة عليه وحضور دفنه
chapter_en: The Book of Visiting the Sick
ar:
عن أبي رافع أسلم مولي رسول الله صلى  الله عليه وسلم  أن رسول الله صلى  الله عليه وسلم  قال‏:‏ ‏
"‏ من غسل ميتاً فكتم عليه ، غفر الله له أربعين مرة‏"‏ ‏(‏‏(‏رواه الحاكم وقال صحيح علي شرط مسلم‏)‏‏)‏‏.‏

isnad_chain.name_ar: أبي رافع أسلم مولي رسول الله

sunnah_ref_en:
Abu Rafi` Aslam (May Allah be pleased with him), the freed slave of the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) reported: The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said, "He who washes a dead body and conceals what he notices of physical defects, he will be forgiven forty times.''

.
iwh_en:
Abu Rafi` Aslam (may Allah be pleased with him), the freed slave of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ reported: the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "He who washes a dead body and conceals what he notices of physical defects, he will be forgiven forty times.''

.
iwh_id:
Abu Rafi` Aslam (radhiyallahu 'anhu), the freed slave of Rasulullah ﷺ meriwayatkan: Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda, "He who washes a dead body dan conceals what he notices of physical defects, he will be forgiven forty times.''

.

chapter_ar: كتاب عيادة المريض وتشييع الميت والصلاة عليه وحضور دفنه
chapter_en: The Book of Visiting the Sick
ar: عن أبي هريرة رضي الله عنه قال‏:‏ قال رسول الله صلى  الله عليه وسلم ‏:‏ “من شهد الجنازة حتي يصلى  عليها، فله قيراط ، ومن شهدها حتي تدفن، فله قيراطان‏"‏ قيل‏:‏ وما القيراطان‏؟‏ قال‏:‏ “مثل الجبلين العظيمين” ‏(‏‏(‏متفق عليه‏)‏‏)‏ ‏.‏
ar_clean: عن أبي هريرة رضي الله عنه قال: قال رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم : “من شهد الجنازة حتي يصلى عليها، فله قيراط ، ومن شهدها حتي تدفن، فله قيراطان" قيل: وما القيراطان؟ قال: “مثل الجبلين العظيمين” ((متفق عليه)) .

isnad_chain.name_en: Abu Hurairah [may Allah be pleased with him]

sunnah_ref_en:
Abu Hurairah (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said, "Whoever follows the funeral procession and offers the funeral prayer for it, will get a reward equal to one Qirat, and whoever attends it till burial, will get a reward equal to two Qirat.'' It was asked, "What are two Qirat?'' He (ﷺ) replied, "Equal to two huge mountains."

.
iwh_en:
Abu Hurairah (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "Whoever follows the funeral procession and offers the funeral prayer for it, will get a reward equal to one Qirat, and whoever attends it till burial, will get a reward equal to two Qirat.'' It was asked, "What are two Qirat?'' He (ﷺ) replied, "Equal to two huge mountains."

.
iwh_id:
Abu Hurairah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda, "Whoever follows the funeral procession dan offers the funeral prayer for it, will get a reward equal to one Qirat, and whoever attends it till burial, will get a reward equal to two Qirat.'' It was bertanya, "What are two Qirat?'' He (ﷺ) menjawab, "Equal to two huge mountains."

.

chapter_ar: كتاب عيادة المريض وتشييع الميت والصلاة عليه وحضور دفنه
chapter_en: The Book of Visiting the Sick
ar: وعنه أن رسول الله صلى  الله عليه وسلم  قال‏:‏ ‏"‏من اتبع جنازة مسلم إيماناً واحتساباً، وكان معه حتي يصلى  عليها ويفرغ من دفنها، فإنه يرجع من الأجر بقيراطين كل قيراط مثل أحدٍ، ومن صلى  عليها، ثم رجع قبل أن تدفن ، فإنه يرجع بقيراط” ‏(‏‏(‏رواه البخاري‏)‏‏)‏‏.‏
ar_clean: وعنه أن رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم قال: "من اتبع جنازة مسلم إيماناً واحتساباً، وكان معه حتي يصلى عليها ويفرغ من دفنها، فإنه يرجع من الأجر بقيراطين كل قيراط مثل أحدٍ، ومن صلى عليها، ثم رجع قبل أن تدفن ، فإنه يرجع بقيراط” ((رواه البخاري)).
matn_ar: وعنه أن رسول الله صلى  الله عليه وسلم  قال‏:‏ ‏"‏من اتبع جنازة مسلم إيماناً واحتساباً، وكان معه حتي يصلى  عليها ويفرغ من دفنها، فإنه يرجع من الأجر بقيراطين كل قيراط مثل أحدٍ، ومن صلى  عليها، ثم رجع قبل أن تدفن ، فإنه يرجع بقيراط” ‏(‏‏(‏رواه البخاري‏)‏‏)‏‏.‏
matn_ar_clean: وعنه أن رسول الله صلى  الله عليه وسلم  قال‏:‏ ‏"‏من اتبع جنازة مسلم إيمانا واحتسابا، وكان معه حتي يصلى  عليها ويفرغ من دفنها، فإنه يرجع من الأجر بقيراطين كل قيراط مثل أحد، ومن صلى  عليها، ثم رجع قبل أن تدفن ، فإنه يرجع بقيراط” ‏(‏‏(‏رواه البخاري‏)‏‏)‏‏.‏
sunnah_ref_en: Abu Hurairah (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said, "Whosoever attends the funeral of a Muslim believing and hoping for the reward from Allah and remains with it until the prayer is offered over it and the burial is completed, he will return with a reward of two Qirat; each Qirat is equivalent to Mount Uhud; and whosoever offers his prayer over it and returns before its burial, he will come back with one Qirat.".
iwh_en: Abu Hurairah (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "Whosoever attends the funeral of a Muslim believing and hoping for the reward from Allah and remains with it until the prayer is offered over it and the burial is completed, he will return with a reward of two Qirat; each Qirat is equivalent to Mount Uhud; and whosoever offers his prayer over it and returns before its burial, he will come back with one Qirat.".
iwh_id: Abu Hurairah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda, "Whosoever attends the funeral of a Muslim believing dan hoping for the reward from Allah dan remains with it hingga the prayer is offered over it dan the burial is completed, he will return with a reward of two Qirat; each Qirat is equivalent to Mount Uhud; dan whosoever offers his prayer over it dan returns sebelum its burial, he will come back with one Qirat.".

‏"‏ومعناه ‏"‏ ولم يشدد في النهي كما يشدد في المحرمات‏.‏

chapter_ar: كتاب عيادة المريض وتشييع الميت والصلاة عليه وحضور دفنه
chapter_en: The Book of Visiting the Sick
ar: عن عائشة رضي الله عنها، قالت: قال رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم: “ما من ميت يصلي عليه أمة من المسلمين يبلغون مائة كلهم يشفعون له إلا شفعوا فيه“ ((رواه مسلم).

sunnah_ref_en:
'Aishah (May Allah be pleased with her) reported: The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said, "If a group of Muslims numbering a hundred perform funeral prayer over a dead person, and all of them ask Allah's forgiveness for him, their prayer for him will be accepted."

.

'Aishah (may Allah be pleased with her) reported: the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "If a group of Muslims numbering a hundred perform funeral prayer over a dead person, and all of them ask Allah's forgiveness for him, their prayer for him will be accepted."

.

'Aishah (radhiyallahu 'anha) meriwayatkan:
Rasulullah ﷺ berkata, "If a group of Muslims numbering a hundred perform funeral shalat over a dead person, and all of them ask Allah's ampunan for him, their shalat for him will be accepted."

.

chapter_ar: كتاب عيادة المريض وتشييع الميت والصلاة عليه وحضور دفنه
chapter_en: The Book of Visiting the Sick
ar:
وعن ابن عباس رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنهُما قال سمعت رَسُول اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيهِ وَسَلَّم يقول : 
"ما من رجل مسلم يموت فيقوم على جنازته أربعون رجلاً لا يشركون بالله شيئاً إلا شفعهم اللَّه فيه." ((رَوَاهُ مُسلِمٌ)).

عليه وسلم يقول : 
"ما من رجل مسلم يموت فيقوم على جنازته أربعون رجلا لا يشركون بالله شيئا إلا شفعهم الله فيه." ((رواه مسلم)).
sunnah_ref_en: Ibn 'Abbas (May Allah be pleased with them) reported: The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said, "If a Muslim dies and forty people, who do not associate anything with Allah in worship, participate in the funeral prayer over him, Allah will accept their intercession for him.".
iwh_en: Ibn 'Abbas (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "If a Muslim dies and forty people, who do not associate anything with Allah in worship, participate in the funeral prayer over him, Allah will accept their intercession for him.".
iwh_id: Ibn 'Abbas (radhiyallahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda, "If a Muslim dies dan forty orang-orang, who jangan associate anything with Allah in worship, participate in the funeral prayer over him, Allah will accept their intercession for him.".

chapter_ar: كتاب عيادة المريض وتشييع الميت والصلاة عليه وحضور دفنه
chapter_en: The Book of Visiting the Sick
ar: وعن مرثد بن عبد الله اليزني قال‏:‏ كان مالك بن هبيرة رضي الله عنه إذا صلى  علي الجنازة ، فتقال الناس عليها، جزأهم عليها ثلاثة أجزاء، ثم قال‏:‏ قال رسول الله صلى  الله عليه وسلم ‏:‏ “من صلى  عليه ثلاثة صفوف، فقد أوجب “ ‏(‏‏(‏رواه أبو دواد، والترمذي وقال‏:‏ حديث حسن‏)‏‏)‏‏.‏

sunnah_ref_en: Marthad bin 'Abdullah Al-Yazni reported: When Malik bin Hubairah (May Allah be pleased with him) offered a funeral prayer and found a small number of participants, he would divide them into three rows and would say that the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said, "If three rows of men offer funeral prayer over anyone, his entry into Jannah will become certain.".
iwh_en: Marthad bin 'Abdullah Al-Yazni (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that when Malik bin Hubairah (may Allah be pleased with him) offered a funeral prayer and found a small number of participants, he would divide them into three rows and would say that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "If three rows of men offer funeral prayer over anyone, his entry into Jannah will become certain.".
iwh_id: Marthad bin 'Abdullah Al-Yazni (radhiyallahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan bahwa when Malik bin Hubairah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) offered a funeral shalat dan menemukan a kecil number of participants, ia divide them into three rows dan would say that Rasulullah ﷺ berkata, "If three rows of men offer funeral shalat over anyone, his entry into Jannah will become certain.".

chapter_ar: كتاب عيادة المريض وتشييع الميت والصلاة عليه وحضور دفنه
chapter_en: The Book of Visiting the Sick
ar: عن أبي عبد الرحمن بن عوف بن مالك رضي الله عنه قال‏:‏ صلى  رسول الله صلى  الله عليه وسلم  علي جنازة، فحفظت من دعائه وهو يقول‏:‏ ‏"‏اللهم اغفر له، وارحمه، وعافه، واعف عنه، وأكرم نزله، ووسع مدخله واغسله بالماء والثلج والبرد ونقه من الخطايا، كما نقيت الثوب الأبيض من الدنس، وأبدله داراً خيراً من داره، وأهلاً خيراً من أهله، وزوجاً خيراً من زوجه، وأدخله الجنة، وأعذه من عذاب القبر، ومن عذاب النار” حتي تمنين أن أكون ذلك الميت‏.‏ ‏(‏‏(‏رواه مسلم‏)‏‏)‏‏.‏

sunnah_ref_en: Abu 'Abdur-Rahman 'Auf bin Malik (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) offered a funeral prayer and I memorized his supplication. He (ﷺ) prayed: "Allahummaghfir lahu, warhamhu, wa 'afihi, wa'fu 'anhu, wa akrim nuzulahu, wa wassi' mudkhalahu, waghsilhu bil-ma'i wath-thalji wal-baradi, wa naqqihi minal-khataya, kama naqqaytath-thawbal-abyada minad-danasi, wa abdilhu daran khairan min darihi, wa ahlan khairan min ahlihi, wa zawjan khairan min zawjihi, wa adkhilhul-Jannata, wa a'idh-hu min 'adhabil- qabri, wa min 'adhabin-nar .

He (ﷺ) prayed: "Allahummaghfir lahu, warhamhu, wa 'afihi, wa'fu 'anhu, wa akrim nuzulahu, wa wassi' mudkhalahu, waghsilhu bil-ma'i wath-thalji wal-baradi, wa naqqihi minal-khataya, kama naqqaytath-thawbal-abyada minad-danasi, wa abdilhu daran khairan min darihi, wa ahlan khairan min ahlihi, wa zawjan khairan min zawjihi, wa adkhilhul-Jannata, wa a'idh-hu min 'adhabil- qabri, wa min 'adhabin-nar .
iwh_id: Abu 'Abdur-Rahman 'Auf bin Malik (radhiyallahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ offered a funeral prayer dan aku memorized his supplication. He (ﷺ) prayed: "Allahummaghfir lahu, warhamhu, wa 'afihi, wa'fu 'anhu, wa akrim nuzulahu, wa wassi' mudkhalahu, waghsilhu bil-ma'i wath-thalji wal-baradi, wa naqqihi minal-khataya, kama naqqaytath-thawbal-abyada minad-danasi, wa abdilhu daran khairan min darihi, wa ahlan khairan min ahlihi, wa zawjan khairan min zawjihi, wa adkhilhul-Jannata, wa a'idh-hu min 'adhabil- qabri, wa min 'adhabin-nar .

chapter_ar: كتاب عيادة المريض وتشييع الميت والصلاة عليه وحضور دفنه
chapter_en: The Book of Visiting the Sick
ar:
وعن أبي هريرة وأبي قتادة، وأبي إبراهيم الأشهلي عن أبيه -وأبوه صحابي- رضي الله عنهم، عن النبي صلى الله عليه وسلمى الله عليه وسلم الله عليه وسلم الله عليه وسلم  أنه صلى  علي جنازة فقال‏:‏‏
"‏اللهم اغفر لحينا وميتنا، وصغيرنا وكبيرنا، وذكرنا وأنثانا، وشاهدنا وغائبنا‏.‏ اللهم من أحييته منا، فأحيه علي الإسلام، ومن توفيته منا، فتوفه علي الإيمان؛ اللهم لا تحرمنا أجره، ولا تفتنا بعده‏"‏ ‏‏(‏‏(‏رواه الترمذي‏)‏‏)‏
 من رواية أبي هريرة وأبو هريرة صحيح والأشهلي، ورواه أبو داود من رواية أبي هريرة وأبي قتادة‏.‏ قال الحاكم‏:‏ حديث أبي هريرة صحيح علي شرط البخاري ومسلم، قال الترمذي‏:‏ قال البخاري‏:‏ أصح روايات هذا الحديث رواية الأشهلي‏.‏ قال البخاري‏:‏ وأصح شيء في الباب حديث عوف بن مالك‏.‏

isnad_chain.name_en: Abu Hurairah [may Allah be pleased with him]

sunnah_ref_en:
Abu Hurairah, Abu Qatadah, and Abu Ibrahim Al-Ash-hali (May Allah be pleased with them) reported: The Prophet (ﷺ) offered a funeral prayer and said: "Allahummaghfir lihaiyina wa maiyitina, wa saghirina wa kabirina, wa dhakarina wa unthana, wa shahidina wa gha'ibina. Allahumma man ahyaiytahu minna, fa'ahyihi 'alal-Islam, wa man tawaffaiytahu minna, fatawaffahu 'alal-Iman. Allahumma la tahrimna ajrahu, wa la taftinna ba'dahu (O Allah, forgive our living and our dead, our present and our absent, our young and our old, our male and our female. O Allah, whosoever of us You keep alive, keep him alive (faithful) to Islam, and whosoever of us You cause to die, let him die having Iman. O Allah, do not deprive us of our reward (for being patient) and do not subject us to trials after his death."

.
iwh_en:
Abu Hurairah, Abu Qatadah, and Abu Ibrahim Al-Ash-hali (may Allah be pleased with them) reported: the Prophet ﷺ offered a funeral prayer and said: "Allahummaghfir lihaiyina wa maiyitina, wa saghirina wa kabirina, wa dhakarina wa unthana, wa shahidina wa gha'ibina. Allahumma man ahyaiytahu minna, fa'ahyihi 'alal-Islam, wa man tawaffaiytahu minna, fatawaffahu 'alal-Iman. Allahumma la tahrimna ajrahu, wa la taftinna ba'dahu (O Allah, forgive our living and our dead, our present and our absent, our young and our old, our male and our female. O Allah, whosoever of us You keep alive, keep him alive (faithful) to Islam, and whosoever of us You cause to die, let him die having Iman. O Allah, do not deprive us of our reward (for being patient) and do not subject us to trials after his death."

.
iwh_id:
Abu Hurairah, Abu Qatadah, and Abu Ibrahim Al-Ash-hali (radhiyallahu 'anhum) meriwayatkan: Nabi ﷺ offered a funeral prayer dan berkata: "Allahummaghfir lihaiyina wa maiyitina, wa saghirina wa kabirina, wa dhakarina wa unthana, wa shahidina wa gha'ibina. Allahumma man ahyaiytahu minna, fa'ahyihi 'alal-Islam, wa man tawaffaiytahu minna, fatawaffahu 'alal-Iman. Allahumma la tahrimna ajrahu, wa la taftinna ba'dahu (Ya Allah, forgive our living dan our dead, our present dan our absent, our muda dan our tua, our male dan our female. Ya Allah, whosoever of us You menjaga alive, menjaga him alive (faithful) to Islam, and whosoever of us You cause to die, hendaklah ia die having Iman. Ya Allah, jangan deprive us of our reward (for being patient) and jangan subject us to trials setelah his death."

.

chapter_ar: كتاب عيادة المريض وتشييع الميت والصلاة عليه وحضور دفنه
chapter_en: The Book of Visiting the Sick
ar:
وعن عبد الله بن أبي أوفي رضي الله عنهما أنه كبر علي جنازة ابنة له أربع تكبيرات، فقام بعد الرابعة كقدر ما بين التكبيرتين يستغفر لها ويدعو، ثم قال‏:‏ كان رسول الله صلى  الله عليه وسلم  يصنع هكذا‏.‏ 
وفي رواية‏:‏ ‏ ‏كبر أربعاً، فمكث ساعة حتي ظننت أنه سيكبر خمساً، ثم سلم عن يمينه وعن شماله‏.‏ فلما انصرف قلنا له‏:‏ ما هذا‏؟‏ فقال‏:‏ إني لا أزيدكم علي ما رأيت رسول الله صلى  الله عليه وسلم  يصنع، أو‏:‏ هكذا صنع رسول الله صلى  الله عليه وسلم ‏ ‏ ‏(‏‏(‏رواه الحاكم وقال‏:‏ حديث صحيح‏)‏‏)‏‏.‏

فقام بعد الرابعة كقدر ما بين التكبيرتين يستغفر لها ويدعو، ثم قال: كان رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم يصنع هكذا. 
وفي رواية: كبر أربعاً، فمكث ساعة حتي ظننت أنه سيكبر خمساً، ثم سلم عن يمينه وعن شماله. فلما انصرف قلنا له: ما هذا؟ فقال: إني لا أزيدكم علي ما رأيت رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم يصنع، أو: هكذا صنع رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم ((رواه الحاكم وقال: حديث صحيح)).

وفي رواية: كبر أربعا، فمكث ساعة حتي ظننت أنه سيكبر خمسا، ثم سلم عن يمينه وعن شماله. فلما انصرف قلنا له: ما هذا؟ فقال: إني لا أزيدكم علي ما رأيت رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم يصنع، أو: هكذا صنع رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم ((رواه الحاكم وقال: حديث صحيح)).
sunnah_ref_en: 'Abdullah bin Abu Aufa (May Allah be pleased with them) reported: While offering the funeral prayer of one of his daughters, he recited four Takbir, and after the fourth Takbir he continued standing for a time equal to an interval between two Takbir, praying for her and seeking Allah's forgiveness for her." Then he said: "The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) used to do so."Another narration is: He (Abdullah) recited four Takbir and remained standing in prayer for some time till we thought that he would recite the fifth Takbir. Then he gave Salam on the right and on the left. When he turned aside, we asked him about it. He replied: "I would add nothing to what I saw the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) doing," or he said: "The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) used to do so.".
iwh_en: 'Abdullah bin Abu Aufa (may Allah be pleased with them) reported: While offering the funeral prayer of one of his daughters, he recited four Takbir, and after the fourth Takbir he continued standing for a time equal to an interval between two Takbir, praying for her and seeking Allah's forgiveness for her." Then he said: "the Messenger of Allah ﷺ used to do so."Another narration is: He (Abdullah) recited four Takbir and remained standing in prayer for some time till we thought that he would recite the fifth Takbir. Then he gave Salam on the right and on the left. When he turned aside, we asked him about it. He replied: "I would add nothing to what I saw the Messenger of Allah ﷺ doing," or he said: "the Messenger of Allah ﷺ used to do so.".
iwh_id:
'Abdullah bin Abu Aufa (radhiyallahu 'anhum) meriwayatkan:
Sementara offering the funeral shalat of one of his daughters, he recited four Takbir, and setelah the fourth Takbir he continued standing untuk time equal to an interval antara two Takbir, praying for her dan seeking Allah's ampunan for her." Kemudian he berkata: "Rasulullah ﷺ biasa do jadi."Another narration is: He (Abdullah) recited four Takbir dan remained standing in shalat for some time till we berpikir that ia membaca the fifth Takbir. Kemudian he memberikan Salam di kanan dan di meninggalkan. Ketika ia turned aside, we bertanya him tentang it. Ia menjawab: "I would add tidak ada to what Saya melihat Rasulullah ﷺ doing," or he berkata: "Rasulullah ﷺ biasa do jadi.".
