chapter_ar: كتاب عيادة المريض وتشييع الميت والصلاة عليه وحضور دفنه
chapter_en: The Book of Visiting the Sick
ar: وعن أبي سعيد الخدري رضي الله عنه قال‏:‏ كان النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم الله عليه وسلمى الله عليه وسلم  الله عليه وسلم  يقول‏:‏ ‏"‏إذا وضعت الجنازة، فاحتملها الرجال علي أعناقهم، فإن كانت صالحة، قال‏:‏ قدموني، وإن كانت غير صالحة، قالت لأهلها‏:‏ يا ويلها أين تذهبون بها‏؟‏ يسمع صوتها كل شيء إلا الأنسان، ولو سمع الأنسان لصعق” ‏(‏‏(‏رواه البخاري‏)‏‏)‏‏.‏
ar_clean: وعن أبي سعيد الخدري رضي الله عنه قال: كان النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم الله عليه وسلمى الله عليه وسلم الله عليه وسلم يقول: "إذا وضعت الجنازة، فاحتملها الرجال علي أعناقهم، فإن كانت صالحة، قال: قدموني، وإن كانت غير صالحة، قالت لأهلها: يا ويلها أين تذهبون بها؟ يسمع صوتها كل شيء إلا الأنسان، ولو سمع الأنسان لصعق” ((رواه البخاري)).
isnad_ar: وعن أبي سعيد الخدري رضي الله عنه قال: كان النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم الله عليه وسلمى الله عليه وسلم الله عليه وسلم يقول: "إذا وضعت الجنازة
matn_ar: فاحتملها الرجال علي أعناقهم، فإن كانت صالحة، قال: قدموني، وإن كانت غير صالحة، قالت لأهلها: يا ويلها أين تذهبون بها؟ يسمع صوتها كل شيء إلا الأنسان، ولو سمع الأنسان لصعق” ((رواه البخاري)).
matn_ar_clean: فاحتملها الرجال علي أعناقهم، فإن كانت صالحة، قال: قدموني، وإن كانت غير صالحة، قالت لأهلها: يا ويلها أين تذهبون بها؟ يسمع صوتها كل شيء إلا الأنسان، ولو سمع الأنسان لصعق” ((رواه البخاري)).
sunnah_ref_en: Abu Sa'id Al-Khudri (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "When a dead body is placed on a bier and men carry it on their shoulders, if he was a pious man, it (corpse) says: 'Take me in haste.' But if he was not righteous, it says to its bearers: 'Woe to it. Where are you taking it?' Everything except man hears its voice. Had a human being heard its voice, he would have surely fallen into swoon.".
iwh_en: Abu Sa'id Al-Khudri (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that the Prophet ﷺ said, "When a dead body is placed on a bier and men carry it on their shoulders, if he was a pious man, it (corpse) says: 'Take me in haste.' But if he was not righteous, it says to its bearers: 'Woe to it. Where are you taking it?' Everything except man hears its voice. Had a human being heard its voice, he would have surely fallen into swoon.".
iwh_id: Abu Sa'id Al-Khudri (radhiyallahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan bahwa Nabi ﷺ berkata, "When a dead body is placed on a bier dan men carry it on their shoulders, jika ia was a pious man, it (corpse) berkata: 'Take me in haste.' Namun jika ia bukan righteous, it berkata to its bearers: 'Woe to it. Where are you taking it?' Everything except man hears its voice. Had a human being mendengar its voice, ia have surely fallen into swoon.".

chapter_ar: كتاب عيادة المريض وتشييع الميت والصلاة عليه وحضور دفنه

chapter_ar: كتاب عيادة المريض وتشييع الميت والصلاة عليه وحضور دفنه
chapter_en: The Book of Visiting the Sick
ar: وعن حصين بن وحوح رضي الله عنه أن طلحة بن البراء رضي الله عنه مرض، فأتاه النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم الله عليه وسلمى الله عليه وسلم  الله عليه وسلم  يعوده فقال‏:‏ إني لا أري طلحة إلا قد حدث فيه الموت فآذوني به وعجلوا به، فإنه لا ينبغي لجيفة مسلم أن تحبس بين ظهراني أهله‏"‏ ‏(‏‏(‏رواه أبو داود‏)‏‏)‏‏.‏
ar_clean: وعن حصين بن وحوح رضي الله عنه أن طلحة بن البراء رضي الله عنه مرض، فأتاه النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم الله عليه وسلمى الله عليه وسلم الله عليه وسلم يعوده فقال: إني لا أري طلحة إلا قد حدث فيه الموت فآذوني به وعجلوا به، فإنه لا ينبغي لجيفة مسلم أن تحبس بين ظهراني أهله" ((رواه أبو داود)).

sunnah_ref_en: Hussain bin Wahwah (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: When Talhah bin Al-Bara' (May Allah be pleased with him) fell ill, the Prophet (ﷺ) came to visit him and said, "Verily, I think that Talhah is about to die. So inform me when he is dead and make haste (in burying him). Indeed, it is improper for the corpse of a Muslim to be kept lying unburied among his family members.".
iwh_en: Hussain bin Wahwah (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that when Talhah bin Al-Bara' (may Allah be pleased with him) fell ill, the Prophet ﷺ came to visit him and said, "Verily, I think that Talhah is about to die. So inform me when he is dead and make haste (in burying him). Indeed, it is improper for the corpse of a Muslim to be kept lying unburied among his family members.".
iwh_id: Hussain bin Wahwah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan bahwa when Talhah bin Al-Bara' (radhiyallahu 'anhu) fell ill, Nabi ﷺ datang kepada visit him dan berkata, "Verily, I berpikir that Talhah is tentang to die. Jadi inform me ketika ia is dead dan membuat haste (in burying him). Indeed, it is improper untuk corpse dari Muslim to be menjaga lying unburied di antara his family members.".

chapter_ar: كتاب عيادة المريض وتشييع الميت والصلاة عليه وحضور دفنه
chapter_en: The Book of Visiting the Sick
ar:
عن علي رضي الله عنه قال‏:‏ كنا في جنازة في بقيع الغرقد فأتانا رسول الله صلى  الله عليه وسلم  فقعد، وقعدنا حوله ومعه مخصرة فنكس وجعل ينكت بمخصرته، ثم قال‏:‏ ما منكم من أحد إلا وقد كتب مقعده من النار ومقعده من الجنة” فقالوا‏:‏ يا رسول الله أفلا نتكل علي كتابنا‏؟‏ فقال‏:‏ ‏
"‏اعملوا فكل ميسر لما خلق له‏"‏ وذكر تمام الحديث‏.‏ ‏(‏‏(‏متفق عليه‏)‏‏)‏
ar_clean:
عن علي رضي الله عنه قال: كنا في جنازة في بقيع الغرقد فأتانا رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم فقعد، وقعدنا حوله ومعه مخصرة فنكس وجعل ينكت بمخصرته، ثم قال: ما منكم من أحد إلا وقد كتب مقعده من النار ومقعده من الجنة” فقالوا: يا رسول الله أفلا نتكل علي كتابنا؟ فقال: 
"اعملوا فكل ميسر لما خلق له" وذكر تمام الحديث. ((متفق عليه))

sunnah_ref_en:
`Ali (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: We were accompanying a funeral procession in Baqi` Al-Gharqad (graveyard in Al-Madinah) when the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) proceeded towards us and sat down. We sat around him. He had a small stick in his hand. He was bending down his head and scraping the ground with the stick. He said, "There is none among you but has a place assigned for him either in the Jannah or in the Hell." The Companions said: "O Messenger of Allah, should we not depend upon what has been written for us (and give up doing good deeds)?'' The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said, "Carry on doing good deeds. Every one will find it easy to do such deeds (as will lead him to his destined place) for which he has been created."

.
iwh_en:
`Ali (may Allah be pleased with him) reported: We were accompanying a funeral procession in Baqi` Al-Gharqad (graveyard in Al-Madinah) when the Messenger of Allah ﷺ proceeded towards us and sat down. We sat around him. He had a small stick in his hand. He was bending down his head and scraping the ground with the stick. He said, "There is none among you but has a place assigned for him either in the Jannah or in the Hell." The Companions said: "O Messenger of Allah, should we not depend upon what has been written for us (and give up doing good deeds)?'' the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "Carry on doing good deeds. Every one will find it easy to do such deeds (as will lead him to his destined place) for which he has been created."

.
iwh_id:
`Ali (radhiyallahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan: We were accompanying a funeral procession in Baqi` Al-Gharqad (graveyard in Al-Madinah) ketika Rasulullah ﷺ proceeded kepada us dan duduk down. We duduk sekitar him. He had a kecil stick in his hand. He was bending down his head dan scraping the ground with the stick. He bersabda, "There is none di antara you namun has a place assigned for him either in the Jannah or in the Hell." The Companions bersabda: "O Rasulullah, should we not depend upon what has been written for us (and memberikan up doing baik deeds)?'' Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda, "Carry on doing baik deeds. Every one will menemukan it easy to do such deeds (as will lead him to his destined place) for which he has been created."

.

chapter_ar: كتاب عيادة المريض وتشييع الميت والصلاة عليه وحضور دفنه
chapter_en: The Book of Visiting the Sick
ar:
عن أبي عمرو -وقيل‏:‏ أبو عبد الله، وقيل‏:‏ أبو ليلى- عثمان بن عفان رضي الله عنه قال‏:‏ كان النبي صلى الله عليه وسلمى الله عليه وسلم الله عليه وسلم الله عليه وسلم  إذا فرغ من دفن الميت وقف عليه، وقال‏:‏ ‏
"‏استغفروا لأخيكم وسلوا له التثبيت، فإنه الآن يسأل‏"‏ ‏(‏‏(‏رواه أبو داود‏)‏‏)‏‏.‏

"استغفروا لأخيكم وسلوا له التثبيت، فإنه الآن يسأل" ((رواه أبو داود)).

sunnah_ref_en:
Abu `Amr (also called Abu `Abdullah and also Abu Laila) from `Uthman bin `Affan (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: After the burial of a dead man, the Prophet (ﷺ) would stand by the grave and say, "Ask Allah to forgive your brother and supplicate for him for steadfastness because he is being questioned (about his deeds) now."

.
iwh_en:
Abu `Amr (also called Abu `Abdullah and also Abu Laila) from `Uthman bin `Affan (may Allah be pleased with him) reported: After the burial of a dead man, the Prophet ﷺ would stand by the grave and say, "Ask Allah to forgive your brother and supplicate for him for steadfastness because he is being questioned (about his deeds) now."

.
iwh_id:
Abu `Amr (juga called Abu `Abdullah dan juga Abu Laila) from `Uthman bin `Affan (radhiyallahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan: After the burial of a dead man, Nabi ﷺ would berdiri by the grave dan berkata, "Ask Allah to forgive your brother dan supplicate for him for steadfastness because he is being questioned (tentang his deeds) now."

.

chapter_ar: كتاب عيادة المريض وتشييع الميت والصلاة عليه وحضور دفنه
chapter_en: The Book of Visiting the Sick
ar: وعن عمرو بن العاص رضي الله عنه قال‏:‏ إذا دفنتمونى، فأقيموا حول قبري قدر ما تنحر  جذور، ويقسم لحمها حت أستأنس بكم، وأعلم ماذا أراجع به رسل ربي‏"‏ ‏(‏‏(‏رواه مسلم‏.‏ وقد سبق بطوله‏)‏‏)‏‏.‏ وقال الشافعي رحمه الله‏:‏ ويستحب أن يقرأ عنده شيء من القرآن، وإن ختموا القرآن عنده كان حسناً‏.‏
ar_clean: وعن عمرو بن العاص رضي الله عنه قال: إذا دفنتمونى، فأقيموا حول قبري قدر ما تنحر جذور، ويقسم لحمها حت أستأنس بكم، وأعلم ماذا أراجع به رسل ربي" ((رواه مسلم. وقد سبق بطوله)). وقال الشافعي رحمه الله: ويستحب أن يقرأ عنده شيء من القرآن، وإن ختموا القرآن عنده كان حسناً.
isnad_ar: وعن عمرو بن العاص رضي الله عنه قال: إذا دفنتمونى
matn_ar: فأقيموا حول قبري قدر ما تنحر جذور، ويقسم لحمها حت أستأنس بكم، وأعلم ماذا أراجع به رسل ربي" ((رواه مسلم. وقد سبق بطوله)). وقال الشافعي رحمه الله: ويستحب أن يقرأ عنده شيء من القرآن، وإن ختموا القرآن عنده كان حسناً.
matn_ar_clean: فأقيموا حول قبري قدر ما تنحر جذور، ويقسم لحمها حت أستأنس بكم، وأعلم ماذا أراجع به رسل ربي" ((رواه مسلم. وقد سبق بطوله)). وقال الشافعي رحمه الله: ويستحب أن يقرأ عنده شيء من القرآن، وإن ختموا القرآن عنده كان حسنا.
sunnah_ref_en: 'Amr bin Al-'as (May Allah be pleased with him) used to say: "When you have buried me, keep standing near my grave till (for the time it takes to) a camel is slaughtered and its meat is distributed, so that I may feel your nearness and know what to reply to the angels sent by my Rubb.".
iwh_en: 'Amr bin Al-'as (may Allah be pleased with him) used to say: "When you have buried me, keep standing near my grave till (for the time it takes to) a camel is slaughtered and its meat is distributed, so that I may feel your nearness and know what to reply to the angels sent by my Rubb.".
iwh_id: 'Amr bin Al-'as (radhiyallahu 'anhu) biasa berkata: "When you have buried me, menjaga standing near my grave till (for the time it takes to) a camel is slaughtered dan its meat is distributed, sehingga I may feel your nearness dan mengetahui what to reply to the angels sent by my Rubb.".

chapter_ar: كتاب عيادة المريض وتشييع الميت والصلاة عليه وحضور دفنه
chapter_en: The Book of Visiting the Sick
ar:
وعن عائشة رضي الله عنها أن رجلاً قال للنبي صلى  الله عليه وسلم ‏:‏ إن أمي افتلتت نفسها وأراها لو تكلمت، تصدقت، فهل لها أجر إن تصدقت عنها‏؟‏ قال‏:‏ ‏
"‏نعم‏"‏ ‏(‏‏(‏متفق عليه‏)‏‏)‏

sunnah_ref_en: 'Aishah (May Allah be pleased with her) reported: A man said to the Prophet (ﷺ): "My mother has died suddenly. I think that if she were able to talk (alive) she would have given in Sadaqah (charity). So, if I give Sadaqah now on her behalf, will she get the reward?" The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said, "Yes (she will be rewarded for that).".
iwh_en: 'Aishah (may Allah be pleased with her) reported: A man said to the Prophet ﷺ: "My mother has died suddenly. I think that if she were able to talk (alive) she would have given in Sadaqah (charity). So, if I give Sadaqah now on her behalf, will she get the reward?" the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "Yes (she will be rewarded for that).".
iwh_id:
'Aishah (radhiyallahu 'anha) meriwayatkan:
A man berkata to Nabi ﷺ: "My mother has died tiba-tiba. I berpikir that jika ia were able to talk (alive) ia have given in Sadaqah (sedekah). Jadi, if I memberikan Sadaqah now on her behalf, will she get the reward?" Rasulullah ﷺ berkata, "Yes (she will be rewarded for that).".

chapter_ar: كتاب عيادة المريض وتشييع الميت والصلاة عليه وحضور دفنه
chapter_en: The Book of Visiting the Sick
ar: وعن أبي هريرة رضي الله عنه أن رسول الله صلى  الله عليه وسلم  قال‏:‏ “إذا مات الأنسان انقطع عمله إلا من ثلاث‏:‏ صدقة جارية، أو علم ينتفع به، أو ولد صالح يدعو له‏"‏ ‏(‏‏(‏رواه مسلم‏)‏‏)‏‏.‏

isnad_chain.name_en: Abu Hurairah [may Allah be pleased with him]

sunnah_ref_en: Abu Hurairah (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said, "When a man dies, his deeds come to an end, except for three: A continuous charity, knowledge by which people derive benefit, pious son who prays for him.".
iwh_en: Abu Hurairah (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "When a man dies, his deeds come to an end, except for three: A continuous charity, knowledge by which people derive benefit, pious son who prays for him.".
iwh_id: Abu Hurairah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda, "When seorang laki-laki dies, his deeds come to an end, except for three: A continuous charity, knowledge by which orang-orang derive benefit, pious son who prays for him.".

chapter_ar: كتاب عيادة المريض وتشييع الميت والصلاة عليه وحضور دفنه
chapter_en: The Book of Visiting the Sick
ar: عن أنس رضي الله عنه قال‏:‏ مروا بجنازة، فأثنوا عليها خيراً، فقال النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم الله عليه وسلمى الله عليه وسلم  الله عليه وسلم ‏:‏ ‏"‏وجبت”، ثم مروا بأخرى، فأثنوا عليها شراً، فقال النبي صلى الله عليه وسلمى الله عليه وسلم الله عليه وسلم الله عليه وسلم ‏:‏ ‏"‏وجبت‏"‏ فقال عمر بن الخطاب رضي الله عنه‏:‏ ما وجبت‏؟‏ قال‏:‏ ‏"‏هذا أثنيتم عليه خيراً، فوجبت له الجنة، وهذا أثنيتم عليه شراً فوجبت عليه النار، أنتم شهداء الله في الأرض” ‏(‏‏(‏متفق عليه‏)‏‏)‏‏.‏

sunnah_ref_en:
Anas (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: Some Companions happened to pass by a funeral procession (bier) and they praised him (the deceased). The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "He will certainly enter it." Then they passed by another funeral procession and they spoke ill of the deceased. The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "He will certainly enter it." `Umar bin Al-Khattab (May Allah be pleased with him) said: "(O Messenger of Allah,) what do you mean by `He will certainly enter it?'' He (ﷺ) replied, "You praised the first person, so he will enter Jannah; and you spoke ill of the second person, so he will enter Hell. You are Allah's witnesses on earth."

.
iwh_en:
Anas (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that some Companions happened to pass by a funeral procession (bier) and they praised him (the deceased). the Prophet ﷺ said, "He will certainly enter it." Then they passed by another funeral procession and they spoke ill of the deceased. the Prophet ﷺ said, "He will certainly enter it." `Umar bin Al-Khattab (may Allah be pleased with him) said: "(O Messenger of Allah,) what do you mean by `He will certainly enter it?'' He (ﷺ) replied, "You praised the first person, so he will enter Jannah; and you spoke ill of the second person, so he will enter Hell. You are Allah's witnesses on earth."

.
iwh_id:
Anas (radhiyallahu 'anhu) melaporkan bahwa some Companions happened to pass by a funeral procession (bier) dan mereka praised him (the deceased). Nabi ﷺ berkata, "He will certainly masuk ke it." Kemudian they melewati another funeral procession dan mereka berbicara ill of the deceased. Nabi ﷺ berkata, "He will certainly masuk ke it." `Umar bin Al-Khattab (radhiyallahu 'anhu) berkata: "(O Rasulullah,) what do you mean by `He will certainly masuk ke it?'' He (ﷺ) menjawab, "You praised the pertama person, jadi he will masuk ke Jannah; dan you berbicara ill of the second person, jadi he will masuk ke Neraka. You are Allah's witnesses on earth."

.

chapter_ar: كتاب عيادة المريض وتشييع الميت والصلاة عليه وحضور دفنه
chapter_en: The Book of Visiting the Sick
ar: وعن أبي الأسود قال‏:‏ قدمت المدينة، فجلست إلي عمر بن الخطاب رضي الله عنه فمرت بهم جنازة، فأثني علي صاحبها خيراً فقال عمر‏:‏ وجبت، ثم مر بأخرى، فأثني علي صاحبها خيراً، فقال عمر‏:‏ وجبت، ثم مر بالثالثة، فأثني علي صاحبها شراً، فقال عمر‏:‏ وجبت‏:‏ قال أبو الأسود‏:‏ فقلت‏:‏ وما وجبت يا أمير المؤمنين‏؟‏ قال‏:‏ قلت كما قال النبي صلى الله عليه وسلمى الله عليه وسلم  الله عليه وسلم ‏:‏ “أيما مسلم شهد له أربعة بخير، أدخله الله الجنة‏:‏ فقلنا‏:‏ وثلاثة‏؟‏ قال‏:‏ “وثلاثة‏"‏ فقلنا‏:‏ واثنان‏؟‏ قال‏:‏ ‏"‏واثنان‏"‏ ثم لم نسأله عن الواحد‏"‏ ‏(‏‏(‏رواه البخاري‏)‏‏)‏‏.‏

matn_ar_clean: فجلست إلي عمر بن الخطاب رضي الله عنه فمرت بهم جنازة، فأثني علي صاحبها خيرا فقال عمر: وجبت، ثم مر بأخرى، فأثني علي صاحبها خيرا، فقال عمر: وجبت، ثم مر بالثالثة، فأثني علي صاحبها شرا، فقال عمر: وجبت: قال أبو الأسود: فقلت: وما وجبت يا أمير المؤمنين؟ قال: قلت كما قال النبي صلى الله عليه وسلمى الله عليه وسلم الله عليه وسلم : “أيما مسلم شهد له أربعة بخير، أدخله الله الجنة: فقلنا: وثلاثة؟ قال: “وثلاثة" فقلنا: واثنان؟ قال: "واثنان" ثم لم نسأله عن الواحد" ((رواه البخاري)).
sunnah_ref_en: Abul-Aswad (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: I came to Al- Madinah, and while I was sitting beside 'Umar bin Al-Khattab, a funeral procession passed by. The people praised the deceased, and 'Umar bin Al-Khattab said: "He will certainly enter it." Then another funeral procession passed by and the people praised the deceased. 'Umar bin Al-Khattab (May Allah be pleased with him) said: "He will certainly enter it." A third funeral procession passed by and the people spoke ill of the deceased. He said: "He will certainly enter it." I (Abul-Aswad) asked: "O Amir Al-Mu'minin (i.e., Leader of the Believers)! What do you mean by 'He will certainly enter it'?" He replied: "I said the same as was said by the Prophet (ﷺ). He (ﷺ) said, 'If four persons testify the righteousness of a Muslim, Allah will grant him Jannah.' We asked: 'If three persons testify his righteousness?' He (ﷺ) replied, 'Even three'. Then we asked: 'If two?' He (ﷺ) replied, 'Even two.' We did not ask him (regarding the testimony) of one.".
iwh_en: Abul-Aswad (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that i came to Al- Madinah, and while I was sitting beside 'Umar bin Al-Khattab, a funeral procession passed by. The people praised the deceased, and 'Umar bin Al-Khattab said: "He will certainly enter it." Then another funeral procession passed by and the people praised the deceased. 'Umar bin Al-Khattab (may Allah be pleased with him) said: "He will certainly enter it." A third funeral procession passed by and the people spoke ill of the deceased. He said: "He will certainly enter it." I (Abul-Aswad) asked: "O Amir Al-Mu'minin (i.e., Leader of the Believers)! What do you mean by 'He will certainly enter it'?" He replied: "I said the same as was said by the Prophet ﷺ. He (ﷺ) said, 'If four persons testify the righteousness of a Muslim, Allah will grant him Jannah.' We asked: 'If three persons testify his righteousness?' He (ﷺ) replied, 'Even three'. Then we asked: 'If two?' He (ﷺ) replied, 'Even two.' We did not ask him (regarding the testimony) of one.".
iwh_id: Abul-Aswad (radhiyallahu 'anhu) melaporkan bahwa i datang kepada Al- Madinah, and while I was sitting beside 'Umar bin Al-Khattab, a funeral procession melewati. The orang-orang praised the deceased, and 'Umar bin Al-Khattab berkata: "He will certainly masuk ke it." Kemudian another funeral procession melewati dan orang-orang praised the deceased. 'Umar bin Al-Khattab (radhiyallahu 'anhu) berkata: "He will certainly masuk ke it." A third funeral procession melewati dan orang-orang berbicara ill of the deceased. He berkata: "He will certainly masuk ke it." I (Abul-Aswad) bertanya: "O Amir Al-Mu'minin (i.e., Leader of the Believers)! What do you mean by 'He will certainly masuk ke it'?" Ia menjawab: "Aku berkata the same as was berkata by Nabi ﷺ. He (ﷺ) berkata, 'If four orang-orang testify the righteousness of a Muslim, Allah will grant him Jannah.' Kami bertanya: 'If three orang-orang testify his righteousness?' He (ﷺ) menjawab, 'Even three'. Kemudian we bertanya: 'If two?' He (ﷺ) menjawab, 'Even two.' We tidak ask him (tentang testimony) of one.".

chapter_ar: كتاب عيادة المريض وتشييع الميت والصلاة عليه وحضور دفنه
chapter_en: The Book of Visiting the Sick
ar:
عن أنس رضي الله عنه قال‏:‏ قال رسول الله صلى  الله عليه وسلم ‏:‏ ‏
"‏ما من مسلم يموت له ثلاثة لم يبلغوا الحنث إلا ادخله الله الجنة بفضل رحمته إياهم‏"‏ ‏(‏‏(‏متفق عليه‏)‏‏)‏‏.‏

"ما من مسلم يموت له ثلاثة لم يبلغوا الحنث إلا ادخله الله الجنة بفضل رحمته إياهم" ((متفق عليه)).

matn_ar_clean:

"ما من مسلم يموت له ثلاثة لم يبلغوا الحنث إلا ادخله الله الجنة بفضل رحمته إياهم" ((متفق عليه)).
sunnah_ref_en:
Anas (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said, "Any Muslim who loses three children before they reach the age of maturity, will be granted Jannah by Allah, the Exalted, out of His Mercy for them."

.
iwh_en:
Anas (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "Any Muslim who loses three children before they reach the age of maturity, will be granted Jannah by Allah, the Exalted, out of His Mercy for them."

.
iwh_id:
Anas (radhiyallahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda, "Any Muslim who loses three children sebelum they reach the age of maturity, will be granted Jannah by Allah Subhanahu wa Ta'ala, out of His Mercy for them."

.

chapter_ar: كتاب عيادة المريض وتشييع الميت والصلاة عليه وحضور دفنه
chapter_en: The Book of Visiting the Sick
ar: وعن أبي هريرة رضي الله عنه قال‏:‏ قال رسول الله صلى  الله عليه وسلم ‏:‏ “لا يموت لأحد من المسلمين ثلاثة من الولد لا تمسه النار إلا تحلة القسم‏"‏ ‏(‏‏(‏متفق عليه‏)‏‏)‏

isnad_chain.name_en: Abu Hurairah [may Allah be pleased with him]

sunnah_ref_en: Abu Hurairah (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said, "A Muslim whose three children die (in infancy) will not be touched by the Fire (of Hell) except for the fulfillment of Allah's Oath."
iwh_en: Abu Hurairah (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "A Muslim whose three children die (in infancy) will not be touched by the Fire (of Hell) except for the fulfillment of Allah's Oath."
iwh_id: Abu Hurairah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda, "A Muslim whose three children die (in infancy) tidak akan be touched by the Fire (of Hell) except for the fulfillment of Allah's Oath."

chapter_ar: كتاب عيادة المريض وتشييع الميت والصلاة عليه وحضور دفنه
chapter_en: The Book of Visiting the Sick
ar:
وعن أبي سعيد الخدري رضي الله عنه قال‏:‏ جاءت امرأة إلي رسول الله صلى  الله عليه وسلم ، فقالت‏:‏ يا رسول الله ذهب الرجال بحديثك، فاجعل لنا مننفسك يوماً ناتيك فيه تعلمنا مما علمك الله، قال‏:‏ “اجتمعن يوم كذا وكذا” فاجتمعن، فأتاهن النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم   فعلمهن مما علمه الله، ثم قال‏:‏ ‏
"‏ما منكن من امرأة تقدم ثلاثة من الولد إلا كانوا لها حجاباً من النار‏"‏ فقالت امرأة‏:‏ واثنين‏؟‏ فقال رسول الله صلى  الله عليه وسلم  “واثنين” ‏(‏‏(‏متفق عليه‏)‏‏)‏‏.‏

matn_ar:

"ما منكن من امرأة تقدم ثلاثة من الولد إلا كانوا لها حجابا من النار" فقالت امرأة: واثنين؟ فقال رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم “واثنين” ((متفق عليه)).
sunnah_ref_en: Abu Sa'id Al-Khudri (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: A woman came to the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) and said: "O Messenger of Allah! Only men benefit from your talks, so please fix a day for us, to teach us the knowledge which Allah has taught you." The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) fixed a day and directed them to assemble. When they gathered, the Prophet (ﷺ) went to them and taught them what Allah had taught him. He (ﷺ) then said, "Any woman of you whose three children die (in infancy) they will be a guard for her against Fire (of Hell)." One of the women asked: "What if she loses two?" The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) replied, "Even two.".
iwh_en: Abu Sa'id Al-Khudri (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that a woman came to the Messenger of Allah ﷺ and said: "O Messenger of Allah! Only men benefit from your talks, so please fix a day for us, to teach us the knowledge which Allah has taught you." the Messenger of Allah ﷺ fixed a day and directed them to assemble. When they gathered, the Prophet ﷺ went to them and taught them what Allah had taught him. He (ﷺ) then said, "Any woman of you whose three children die (in infancy) they will be a guard for her against Fire (of Hell)." One of the women asked: "What if she loses two?" the Messenger of Allah ﷺ replied, "Even two.".
iwh_id: Abu Sa'id Al-Khudri (radhiyallahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan bahwa seorang perempuan datang kepada Rasulullah ﷺ dan berkata: "O Rasulullah! Only men benefit from your talks, jadi please fix a day for us, to mengajarkan kami knowledge which Allah has taught you." Rasulullah ﷺ fixed a day dan directed them to assemble. Ketika mereka gathered, Nabi ﷺ pergi kepada them dan taught them what Allah had taught him. He (ﷺ) kemudian berkata, "Any woman of you whose three children die (in infancy) they will be a guard for her terhadap Fire (of Neraka)." One dari women bertanya: "What jika ia loses two?" Rasulullah ﷺ menjawab, "Even two.".

chapter_en: The Book of Visiting the Sick
ar:
عن ابن عمر رضي الله عنهما أن رسول الله صلى  الله عليه وسلم  قال لأصحابه -يعني لما وصلوا الحجر‏:‏ ديار ثمود-‏:‏ ‏"‏لا تدخلوا علي هؤلاء المعذبين إلا أن تكونوا باكين، فإن لم تكونوا باكين، فلا تدخلوا عليهم، لا يصيبكم ما أصابهم” ‏(‏‏(‏متفق عليه‏)‏‏)‏ 
وفي رواية قال‏:‏ لما مر رسول الله صلى  الله عليه وسلم  بالحجر قال‏:‏ ‏"‏لا تدخلوا مساكن الذين ظلموا أنفسهم أن يصيبكم ما أصابهم إلا ان تكونوا باكين‏"‏ ثم قنع رسول الله صلى  الله عليه وسلم  رأسه وأسرع  السير حتي أجاز الوادي‏.‏

sunnah_ref_en:
Narrated Ibn 'Umar: Ibn `Umar (May Allah be pleased with them) reported: When the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) and his Companions reached Al-Hijr, the dwellings of the Thamud people, he admonished his Companions (saying), "Do not pass by these people who are being tormented, without weeping, lest the punishment should befall you as it had befallen them."

.
iwh_en:
Ibn 'Umar (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that ibn `Umar (may Allah be pleased with them) reported: When the Messenger of Allah ﷺ and his Companions reached Al-Hijr, the dwellings of the Thamud people, he admonished his Companions (saying), "Do not pass by these people who are being tormented, without weeping, lest the punishment should befall you as it had befallen them."

.
iwh_id:
Ibn 'Umar (radhiyallahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan bahwa ibn `Umar (radhiyallahu 'anhum) meriwayatkan: Ketika Rasulullah ﷺ dan his Companions tiba di Al-Hijr, the dwellings of the Thamud orang-orang, he admonished his Companions (saying), "Jangan pass by these orang-orang who are being tormented, tanpa weeping, lest the punishment should befall you as it had befallen them."

.
